Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Policy Support: Collaborate with policy officers to Analyse large datasets to provide data-driven actionable recommendations and insights for decision-making.

Data Analysis and Modelling: Conduct exploratory data analysis, including data cleaning if needed, build predictive models, and extract outputs, including insights, from large datasets.

Collaborate with policy colleagues in the team to ensure data availability and quality.

Implement data-driven solutions to optimize business processes and outcomes.

Communicate findings and recommendations to stakeholders in a clear and concise manner.

Ensure the necessary requirements are complied with from the perspective of data scientist users.

Design or participate in the design of data processing algorithms for specific use cases.

Report on the status, risks, and mitigation actions in this respect.

Data outputs including visualization: Create clear and informative data outputs, including visualizations, to communicate complex data findings to stakeholders.

Interaction with the business analysts, customer, users, project leaders and the developers.




LEVEL OF EDUCATION

Bachelor or Master Degree

KNOWLEDGE AND SKILLS:

• Excellent knowledge of data analysis techniques and tools.

• Proficiency (at least 3 years’ experience) with at least 2 of the programming languages R, Stata, Python.

• Statistics and Probability

• Excellent knowledge of statistical concepts needed to expertly conduct complex data analytics.

• Excellent knowledge of distributions and sampling techniques.

• SQL and Database usage, at least 3 years’ experience:

• Intermediate knowledge of SQL (Structured Query Language)

• Intermediate knowledge of data visualization tools such as Power BI or R-Shiny.

• Advanced knowledge of MS Excel spreadsheet editor

Understanding of data governance and data quality principles.

• Ability to deliver efficiently and fast in a changing environment.

• Ability to give business and technical presentations to multiple stakeholders

• Ability to apply high quality standards

• Very good communication skills with technical and non-technical audiences

• Analysis and problem-solving skills

• Capability to write clear and structured technical documents




Following specific expertise is mandatory for the performance of tasks:

• Analytical Mindset: Ability to translate complex data into actionable insights

• Research and Innovation: Stay up to date with the latest advancements in data science and contribute to innovative solutions.




Level : Intermediate

Delivery mode: Near Site (Brussels)

Title: Data Science Internship
HTML:
Job Location

Brussels

Job Description

Do you have passion for technology and desire to learn and grow continuously? Do you want to belong to one of the most advanced Data Science & Analytics group in the industry, developing sophisticated mathematical models and algorithms and applying Operations Research techniques?

Then join P&G as a Data Science intern for Supply Chain! Through your internship, you will build your Data Science and Analytics skills, develop your ability to influence and think strategically and learn how to work across domains and functions to make P&G’s supply chain more performant, more resilient and more sustainable.

Find out more about IT at P&G at www.youtube.com/watch?v=1ial3aeA9SE.

AS A DATA SCIENCE FOR SUPPLY CHAIN INTERN, YOU WILL:

  • Influence decisions from daily through to strategic ones, made across functions, at all levels of leadership, to have a meaningful impact on the performance, resiliency and environmental footprint of one of the largest CPG companies in the world;
  • Build the next generation of supply chain analytics, using:
    • Mathematical modeling techniques: decision analysis, optimization, machine learning, simulation etc.;
    • Programming languages (at least one of these): Python, SQL, R, Hive/Impala, MATLAB, etc.;
    • Data Visualization: Power BI, Tableau, etc.
    • Work with technology partners to translate your innovations into robust, scaled, analytical solutions;
    • Communicate insights from complex data or algorithms into simple conclusions that will empower others to act based on the insights you derive;
    • Belong to an award-winning Analytics / Operations Research community that is global in scope and operates across P&G’s supply chains.

    Job Qualifications

    YOU ARE THE RIGHT FIT IF YOU:

    • Are currently pursuing a Master's degree in a quantitative field (Engineering, Statistics, Operation Research, Decisions Sciences, Computer Science, Applied Math, Economics, Data Science, etc.);
    • Have strong analytical skills and proficiency in at least one programming language (R, Python, SQL, etc.) or family of techniques (optimization, simulation, machine learning, predictive modeling, etc.);
    • Are adaptable and able to handle multiple priorities effectively, both independently and as part of a collaborative team;
    • Are proficient in English, both written and spoken;

    We are offering internships of at least 3 months throughout the year, as of May 2026.

    If studying at a Belgian university, please make sure you consult with the internship department at your school to agree on the paid internship we offer.

    Your Advantages

    • A temporary contract for the duration of your internship, with an attractive compensation package;
    • Monthly allowance: 2366 EUR brut/month, prorated 13th month based on the months worked during your internship period;
    • A flexible benefits plan to customize to your personal needs and kilometer allowance/public transport reduction;
    • Access to Benefits at work platform: A large benefits platform with employee discounts on renowned shops and online commercial platforms as well as on leisure activities and well-known amusement parks;
    • Working in an international and inclusive environment, with diverse and supporting colleagues;
    • Continuous self-development via coaching, trainings and a general Growth Mindset working environment;
    • A vibrant and modern working environment, supporting employees’ wellbeing:
    • Subsidized company restaurant;
    • On-site vibrant Café for breaks and less formal meetings;
    • On-site fitness room and possibility to join regular group sport activities;
    • Flexibility in terms of working hours;
    • Employee Assistance Program: free professional counseling services, work-life resources, and mental health support.
    • Supporting employee's commute: plenty of parking facilities for both cars and bikes, shower facilities, shuttle between Heizel metro and the office, carpooling community.

    ARE YOU EXCITED ABOUT THE ROLE? DEEP DIVE INTO P&G AND CHECK THE BELOW INSIGHTFUL LINKS:

    • Our hiring process consists of three main steps: 1) application, 2) online assessments and 3) interviews. Click here to get all the tips to be fully prepared.
    • P&G is driven to make life better, not just within the company, but across the globe. Click here to check out how we make a meaningful impact on the world.
    • We are the world’s largest consumer goods company. Click here to discover about our iconic brands.

    At P&G #weseeequal

    We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. At P&G we strive to build a culture where everyone feels welcome, included, and able to bring their full selves to work.

    We ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process. Please click here if you require an accommodation during the application process. Please make sure to wait to hear back from us regarding your accommodation before proceeding with the online assessment, we thank you in advance for your patience.

    Job Schedule

    Full time

    Job Number

    R000145698

    Job Segmentation

    Internships

    Starting Pay / Salary Range

    €2.366,00 / month

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Who We Are

Valuement is an international consulting company, part of the Mantu Group, specializing in business process and data analysis. Our mission is to reveal and ensure the long-term competitiveness of our clients through innovative solutions and deep expertise.

Operating in multiple countries, Valuement combines the energy of a young, rapidly growing organization with the professionalism of a reliable global partner. We are committed to creating a dynamic, inclusive, and ambitious work environment where our team members can thrive and grow.




Role Description

The resource will be responsible for managing the entire data analysis lifecycle for clients, handling raw data collection, conversion, enrichment, design, and analysis, up to the development of advanced algorithms. The role involves strong collaboration with internal teams and active contribution to the development of scalable and reliable data analysis solutions.




Your Missions

Lead the end-to-end data analysis process, from raw data discovery to insight and algorithm production
Identify significant patterns, correlations, and trends within large datasets
Work closely with the Data & Business Analysis (DBA) team to understand business needs and translate them into analytical requirements
Communicate and report analysis results to your manager and involved stakeholders
Develop and implement solutions for data analysis automation, ensuring international-scale deployment
Ensure high data quality standards before delivery to internal and external clients




Your Profile

Education

Degree in Computer Science, Mathematics and Statistics, or Information Technology

Experience and Technical Skills

Solid expertise in data analysis
Knowledge of major RDBMS (SQL Server, Oracle, MySQL, PostgreSQL)
Experience using SQL, with basic knowledge of SQL programming (T-SQL or PL/SQL)
Experience developing with R or Python languages considered a plus
Good knowledge of English

Personal Skills

Ability to analyze large and complex datasets
Strong analytical aptitude and problem-solving orientation
High attention to detail

What We Offer

A truly international environment, with colleagues and projects across Europe and beyond
Continuous professional growth opportunities through personalized training programs
A work environment that values collaboration, innovation, and excellence
Access to cutting-edge tools and resources to support your success
A vibrant company culture, with team-building events, CSR initiatives, and much more




Diversity and Inclusion

Valuement is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to promoting diversity and creating an inclusive work environment. For this reason, we welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, creed, age, marital status, disability, or other personal characteristics.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
About Us: At Joveo, we are on a mission to deliver a job for everyone. As the global leader in programmatic job advertising, our Data Science team operates like a hybrid of a librarian and a Swiss army knife—leveraging gargantuan data sets and cutting-edge AI to match the right talent to the right roles.




The Role (TL;DR): We are looking for a Data Scientist to join our cross-functional pods. You will build and deploy state-of-the-art NLP models (like SentenceBERT) and predictive algorithms to optimize job embeddings, occupation classification, and applicant forecasting.




What You’ll Do:

Develop and fine-tune language-agnostic embedding models to process millions of job postings.
Work with transfer learning models and predictive techniques (XGBoost, gradient boosting) to lower Cost-Per-Applicant (CPA) for our clients.
Partner directly with Product Managers and Software Engineers in decentralized, semi-autonomous pods to push code into production.
Identify opportunities to use AI/ML to eliminate bias in recruitment advertising.




What You Bring:

Proven experience in building machine learning models, specifically in NLP and predictive analytics.
Proficiency in Python and relevant libraries (NumPy, Pandas, SciPy, Gensim).
Experience with big data frameworks and deploying models into cloud environments.
A "can-do" mindset and a passion for solving complex, real-world problems.




Why Joveo?

Impact: Your code will directly help millions of people find jobs.
Flexibility: Remote-first culture with flexible working hours.
Growth: Generous learning and development budgets.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
About SailPeak

At Sailpeak, we are a Brussels-based consulting firm specializing in guiding Banking & Insurance firms through the complexities of digital transformation.

With expertise in Business Strategy, Data & AI, and Digital Transformation, Sailpeak helps clients navigate the ever-evolving landscape and reach new heights of success.

🤖 About The Role

We are looking for a Data Scientist with 1-3 years of experience, working on AI-related projects to join our growing team.

In this role, you will drive digital strategy for top-tier banking clients by delivering high-impact projects focused on data analysis, performance optimization, and dashboard creation. You will play a pivotal part in expanding our internal AI capabilities, gaining hands-on experience with cutting-edge machine learning models to build innovative solutions for the financial services sector.

📊 You will:


Assist in data collection, cleansing, and transformation from diverse banking and fintech data sources, ensuring high-quality inputs for advanced analytics.
Contribute to Machine Learning and Deep Learning projects, gaining exposure to AI models, intelligent agents, and digital assistants applied within the financial services sector.
Support the development of data-driven digital strategies and AI-powered campaigns tailored to banking clients.
Conduct A/B testing, hypothesis validation, and performance analysis of digital and AI-driven initiatives in the banking domain.
Create and maintain interactive dashboards, visualizations, and reports to monitor key metrics for digital banking strategies and AI adoption.
Collaborate with cross-functional and agile teams to optimize campaign segmentation, targeting, and personalization, leveraging predictive analytics.
Participate in data interpretation and storytelling, contributing to actionable recommendations that support banking digital transformation and AI initiatives.


📊 You bring to the table:


1-3 years of experience as a Data Scientist
A Master’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Banking, Digital Transformation, or a related field.
A strong eagerness to work with data, AI, and machine learning models, and to grow expertise in digital transformation within banking.
Basic knowledge of data analysis and visualization tools such as Python, SQL, Power BI, Tableau, or VBA.
Familiarity with digital banking platforms, fintech ecosystems, and emerging AI technologies is a plus.
An interest in AI, intelligent agents, and digital assistants, and how they reshape customer journeys and operational efficiency in financial services.
Strong communication and data storytelling skills to translate insights into value for digital strategy and AI adoption.


🗣️ Language Skills

Fluency in English and French/Dutch

🤹 Other Skills


Experience with Scrum or Agile methods is a great plus for us.
We are looking for proactive problem-solvers
You’ll be working independently & autonomously
Passion for growing, building, and continuous learning, especially around the developments within AI and Data Science


🎁 What’s In It For You

💸 A competitive salary and benefits package.

🦾 Working on cutting-edge projects with leading banking clients, in a startup environment

💿 Exposure to a wide array of AI and data science technologies.

🫂 Mentorship from Directors and Partners with backgrounds from renowned companies like Google, PayPal, Semetis, and Sia Partners.

🧑‍🔬 The Process


Step 1: 30-minute call with our Head of Talent (Peter): focusing on your current situation, motivation, experience, and practicalities
Step 2: 45-minute deep dive into the projects you’ve worked on with one of our experienced consultants
Step 3: Onsite Business Case in our office:)
Step 4: Offer!


Are you ready to embark on a rewarding career in management consulting with Sailpeak?

Title: AI/NLP/ML Expert
Description:
COSMOTE Global Solutions NV is seeking an experienced AI/NLP/ML Expert to join our innovative team. As a leading ICT Systems Integrator and member of the OTE Group of Companies, we deliver cutting-edge ICT solutions and services across Cloud, Big Data, Networking, Cybersecurity, and more.

In this role, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and deploying advanced AI, NLP, and Machine Learning models tailored to our business needs. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate intelligent solutions and drive impactful insights from large datasets.

Responsibilities:


Design, implement and maintain a scalable, reliable and secure hybrid cloud ML Ops infrastructure to deploy, test, manage and monitor ML models in different environments;
Development and maintenance of software applications in the field of Natural Language Processing (NLP), Machine Learning (ML), Deep Learning (DL) and/or Artificial Intelligence (AI);
Work closely with data scientists and back-end developers to build, test, integrate and deploy ML models;
Analyse performance metrics and troubleshoot issues to ensure high availability and reliability;
Design CI/CD pipelines, use orchestration solutions and data versioning tools;
Creating automated anomaly detection systems and constant tracking of its performance and optimising ML pipelines for scalability, efficiency and cost-effectiveness.;
Design the IT architecture for solutions in the NLP / ML / AI fields, and coordinate its implementation considering master- and meta-data management concepts;
Provision of security studies, security assessments or other security matters associated with information system projects;
Provision of support and guidance to other team members on MLOps practices


Requirements


Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, or related field
13+ years of experience in IT
Excellent knowledge of managing an on-prem and/or cloud MLOps infrastructure.
Excellent knowledge of containerization and orchestration platforms (e.g. Kubernetes, Docker, Podman, EKS, PKS) Good knowledge of MLflow, TensorFlow (TFX) or equivalents.
Good knowledge of Airflow
Good knowledge of AWS and/or Azure.
Good knowledge of Python.
Good knowledge of Unix and Bash.
Good knowledge agile software development methodologies.
Good knowledge of infrastructure as code (Terraform, CloudFormation)
Good knowledge of messaging services and platforms (e.g. Kafka, Redis, RabbitMQ).
Knowledge of data security measures (knowledge of encryption mechanisms and ML security is considered a plus).
Knowledge of NoSQL databases, such as Elasticsearch, MongoDB, Cassandra, HBase, etc.
Knowledge of query languages, such as SQL, Hive, Pig, etc. and with information extraction.
Experience with data analytics over big datasets, non-structured databases as well as data lakes.
Experience with monitoring and logging tools (e.g. ELK stack, Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry, Cloudwatch)
Experience with model testing and model validation in production environments
Ability to write clear and structured technical documentation
Excellent knowledge of on-prem or cloud solutions for data science applications.
Ability to give business and technical presentations.
Ability to apply high quality standards.
Ability to cope with fast changing technologies.
Very good communication skills with technical and non-technical audiences.
Analysis and problem-solving skills.
Capability to write clear and structured technical documents.
Ability to participate in technical meetings and good communication skills


Optional certifications:


AWS Certified Machine Learning.
Microsoft Azure AI Engineer Associate

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Job Title: Data Scientist

Location: Remote with EU

Contract Type: Freelance / B2B / Contract

Start Date: ASAP

Duration: Until September 2026 initially (Extensions highly likely)




Overview:




We are looking for a hands-on Data Scientist to advance an image analytics platform for industrial turbine inspections. You will help scale and improve the technical infrastructure while creating models to detect anomalies and defects automatically.




Key Responsibilities:




Handle large volumes of inspection data including images, videos, ultrasonic scans, and other sensor-based visual data
Build and maintain data pipelines for collecting, cleaning, organizing, and labeling data for ML model training
Develop and train machine learning and computer vision models
Collaborate with engineers and domain experts to translate inspection insights into model features
Continuously improve model performance and validate results for real-world accuracy
Support deployment of models into production and integration with inspection platforms
Monitor model performance and update models as new data becomes available
Contribute to automating and streamlining inspection processes




Required Skills and Experience:




Strong Python skills
AWS SageMaker experience
Bedrock knowledge
Deployment/MLOps experience is desirable
Strong communication and collaboration skills

  • Continuously improve model performance and validate results for real-world accuracy
  • Support deployment of models into production and integration with inspection platforms
  • Monitor model performance and update models as new data becomes available
  • Contribute to automating and streamlining inspection processes


Required Skills and Experience:


  • Strong Python skills
  • AWS SageMaker experience
  • Bedrock knowledge
  • Deployment/MLOps experience is desirable
  • Strong communication and collaboration skills

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Data Scientist – Shape the Future of Sustainable Energy Innovation!




Join a global leader powering over 16% of the world’s electricity and driving the transition toward a cleaner, more sustainable energy future. Our client is committed to transforming the energy landscape through bold innovation, decarbonization, and next-generation technologies.

As part of their Service domain, you’ll contribute to a growing portfolio of data solutions built on operational insights from gas turbines, maintenance data, and safety incident analytics - creating meaningful impact across global energy systems.




Your Role as a Data Scientist:

Step into a pivotal role where your models, insights, and algorithms directly influence operational excellence, safety, and sustainability. You’ll work across diverse datasets, build advanced machine learning solutions, and collaborate with interdisciplinary teams to bring data-driven intelligence to life within a world-class energy organization.




What You’ll Do:

Develop and maintain machine learning models - from predictive analytics to NLP and computer vision
Explore and analyze large, complex datasets to uncover trends, patterns, and actionable insights
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, build, and deploy ML solutions into production
Develop algorithms and statistical models to enhance performance and accuracy
Communicate insights clearly and persuasively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Stay current with emerging technologies, tools, and best practices in ML and data science




What You Bring:

Strong grounding in ML algorithms (e.g., linear/logistic regression, decision trees, neural networks)
Hands-on experience with AWS SageMaker
Background working closely with Snowflake
Excellent analytical skills with deep expertise in statistical modeling
Strong programming proficiency in Python and SQL, plus familiarity with key ML/analytics libraries
Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau
Strong problem-solving abilities and a structured analytical mindset
Exceptional communication and collaboration skills




Why Join Remobi:

Fully remote, b2b/freelance contracts
Collaborate with world-class talent across Europe’s most exciting sectors
Remobi community access with curated content, role alerts, and networking




👉 Apply today:

Title: Freelance Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency.

Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.

What This Opportunity Involves

While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may:


Design original computational STEM problems that simulate real scientific workflows
Create problems that require Python programming to solve
Ensure problems are computationally intensive and cannot be solved manually within reasonable timeframes (days/weeks)
Develop problems requiring non-trivial reasoning chains and creative problem-solving approaches
Verify solutions using Python with standard libraries (Numpy, Pandas, Scipy, scikit-learn)
Document problem statements clearly and provide verified correct answers


What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit for ML specialists with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:


5+ years of hands-on machine learning experience with proven business impact
Portfolio of completed projects and publications showcasing real-world problem-solving
Expert Python programming for data science (pandas, numpy, scipy, scikit-learn, statsmodels)
Expert statistical analysis and machine learning - deep understanding of algorithms, methods, and their practical applications
Expert with SQL and database operations for data manipulation and analysis
Experience with GenAI technologies (LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering, vector databases)
Understanding of MLOps practices and model deployment workflows
Knowledge of modern frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, LangChain)
Strong written English (C1+)


How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $58 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Sia is a next-generation, global management consulting group. Founded in 1999, we were born digital. Today our strategy and management capabilities are augmented by data science, enhanced by creativity and driven by responsibility. We’re optimists for change and we help clients initiate, navigate and benefit from transformation. We believe optimism is a force multiplier, helping clients to mitigate downside and maximize opportunity. With expertise across a broad range of sectors and services, our 3,000 consultants serve clients worldwide from 48 locations in 19 countries. Our expertise delivers results. Our optimism transforms outcomes.





Job Description



What you will do

As an expert in the field of Machine Learning Engineering you will be responsible for the growth of our Data team, both professionally as well as in size. You will be in charge of shaping and executing the future of your expertise within Sia. You will work on client projects as well as on building our own Sia AI solutions, together with our Dutch team of Data Scientists, our French Data Engineering team, and other business units of Sia Global.



Your responsibilities include:



Leading an internal team towards the development of AI products;
Working in a multidisciplinary team on client projects as an expert on your subject;
Making architectural and design decisions for cloud environments;
Building production level ML/AI solutions with solid software engineering;
Guiding Data Scientists to scale up their models into production (parallelizing, optimizing, tuning, testing, and wrapping);
Giving trainings, leading internal meetings and organizing external events;
Participating in team meetings and workshops with clients;
Writing blog articles, presenting at conferences and sending out press releases on the subject that you lead.

Who you will work with

The Data Science team is young, diverse and growing (10+). You will join a team that will challenge you and invest in your professional development. We have a flat structure and rely on the entrepreneurial spirit of our members: initiative is highly encouraged. We are striving for well-being, diversity and knowledge-sharing inside the team, so that we can grow together.



Who you are



You are an established technical professional that wants to make the next step in their career, taking on more leadership responsibilities.
You have first-rate project management and time management skills.
You are a true problem solver, you turn complex issues into simple and impactful business solutions.
You are enthusiastic for applying Data Science/Engineering solutions to business problems.
You have excellent communication skills and can present complicated concepts in a synthetic way.
You have the ability to adapt rapidly and work on a range of different business areas within a challenging environment.
You have the ability to work collaboratively in a team environment.

In return, you can expect:



An international company that gives importance to diversity, inclusion, employee well-being and corporate social responsibilities actions.
A great working atmosphere with friendly and professional colleagues where innovation and entrepreneurship are encouraged
A personal development plan with a regular follow-up as well training opportunities to develop yourself
A competitive salary with great advantages, like mobility budget, hospital insurance, performance -related bonus,...


Qualifications


MSc level education in Computer Science, Applied Mathematics, Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems or related field of study.
2-4 years of experience in building production-quality ML/Deep Learning pipelines and models.
Good understanding of Machine Learning fundamentals and strong knowledge in a specific domain (e.g., Computer Vision, Deep Learning, NLP).
Expert coding skills in Python and at least one of these languages: Scala, C++, Java.
Hands-on experience with database management languages (e.g., SQL, PostgreSQL).
Hands-on experience in cloud-based infrastructures (AWS/GCP/Azure).
Hands-on experience with Unix-based command line and DevOps tools (Git, Docker, Kubernetes).
Hands-on experience with big data technologies (e.g. Spark, Hadoop, Databricks).
You have a strong interest in data and AI-related topics, the ability to quickly build expertise in these areas, and you’re excited about helping our clients seize the opportunities they offer.
Experience with coaching/mentoring other engineers.
Prior experience in Management Consulting is a strong plus.
Willingness to travel and work at local and international clients.
Fluency in spoken and written English, French and Dutch is required.


Additional Information



Unfortunately, we are unable to provide a work permit for this position.



Sia is an equal opportunity employer. All aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, remuneration, or discipline, are based solely on performance, competence, conduct, or business needs.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
MLOps Engineer

Yearly B2B Contract (Freelance)

Location Brussels-Haren (once a week onsite)




Position Summary

We are looking for an experienced MLOps Engineer to architect and operationalize scalable machine learning infrastructure on Azure within a decentralized data platform environment. You will own the complete ML lifecycle—from development through production—leveraging a hybrid Azure ML and Databricks ecosystem, using infrastructure-as-code practices and MLflow to deliver automated, reliable, and cost-effective ML operations. This role requires building MLOps capabilities that align with data mesh principles, treating data and models as products with clear ownership and domain-driven architecture.




Core Responsibilities

Infrastructure & Automation

Collaborate with cross-functional infrastructure and platform teams to design and deploy production-grade MLOps infrastructure on Azure using Terraform, adhering to data mesh principles of decentralized domain ownership
Work alongside DevOps and platform engineers to build reusable Infrastructure as Code (IaC) templates for ML environments, covering compute resources, storage, networking, and security
Partner with team members to ensure infrastructure is reproducible, version-controlled, and optimized for scalability across multiple domain-oriented data products
Contribute to team efforts in establishing infrastructure standards and best practices for ML workloads
Provision and manage Azure ML workspaces, compute clusters, and related resources alongside Databricks infrastructure

ML Lifecycle Management

Develop automated end-to-end ML pipelines covering training, validation, deployment, and monitoring within a federated data architecture
Implement ML workflows using both Azure ML and Databricks, selecting the appropriate platform based on use case requirements
Implement experiment tracking, model versioning, and artifact management using MLflow integrated with both Azure ML and Databricks environments
Leverage Azure ML's model registry and Databricks MLflow Model Registry for unified model governance across platforms
Manage model promotion workflows across development, staging, and production environments
Design and implement feature store solutions for centralized feature engineering, versioning, and serving across ML workloads
Enable feature reusability and discoverability to support consistent model development across domain teams

Data Mesh & Product Thinking

Build MLOps functionalities within a development data platform following data mesh architecture principles
Apply data-as-a-product mindset to ML models and features, ensuring they meet quality, discoverability, and usability standards
Establish domain-agnostic MLOps capabilities that can be consumed by autonomous domain teams
Implement self-serve ML infrastructure enabling domain teams to independently develop, deploy, and manage models
Define and enforce data product standards including SLAs, data contracts, and quality metrics for ML features and models

Platform Engineering

Configure and optimize both Azure ML compute instances and Azure Databricks clusters for performance and cost efficiency across federated domains
Integrate Azure ML pipelines and Databricks workflows with CI/CD systems to enable seamless, automated model deployments
Establish interoperability between Azure ML and Databricks ecosystems, enabling data scientists to leverage strengths of both platforms
Establish best practices for platform usage and ML workflow orchestration in a decentralized environment
Build feature store infrastructure (Azure ML Feature Store, Databricks Feature Store) that supports cross-domain feature sharing while maintaining domain autonomy

Monitoring & Operations

Build comprehensive monitoring systems to track model performance, data drift, feature quality, and infrastructure health
Implement monitoring solutions that span both Azure ML and Databricks deployments, providing unified observability
Design automated alerting and incident response processes for pipeline failures and degradation
Maintain operational visibility across the full ML stack using observability tools
Implement governance and observability frameworks that provide transparency across domain-owned ML products




Required Qualifications

Cloud & Infrastructure - Hands-on expertise with Azure services including compute, storage, networking, and security tailored for ML workloads - Advanced proficiency in Terraform with proven experience managing complex, multi-environment infrastructure - Demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively with infrastructure and DevOps teams on shared platform initiatives

ML Platform & Tools - Deep knowledge of Azure ML including workspace management, compute resources, pipeline orchestration, model deployment (managed endpoints, AKS), and MLOps capabilities - Deep knowledge of Azure Databricks, including cluster management, job orchestration, and Azure integrations - Experience integrating Azure ML and Databricks ecosystems to create unified ML workflows - Extensive experience with MLflow for experiment tracking, model registry, model serving, and production lifecycle management across both platforms - Proven experience designing and implementing feature stores (Azure ML Feature Store, Databricks Feature Store, or Feast) for online and offline feature serving

Data Mesh & Platform Architecture - Understanding of data mesh principles including domain ownership, data as a product, self-serve data infrastructure, and federated computational governance - Experience building platform capabilities that enable autonomous domain teams while maintaining organizational standards - Ability to design ML systems that support decentralized ownership with centralized governance

Development & Automation - Strong Python programming skills with familiarity in ML frameworks (scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch) and data processing libraries - Demonstrated ability to build CI/CD pipelines for ML systems using Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions, or similar platforms, including automated testing and deployment strategies - Experience with Azure ML SDK/CLI and Databricks APIs for workflow automation

Deployment & Monitoring - Solid understanding of containerization (Docker, Kubernetes) for ML model deployment and scaling - Experience with Azure ML model deployment options including managed endpoints, AKS, and Azure Container Instances - Experience with monitoring and observability platforms such as Azure Monitor, Application Insights, or equivalent tools for tracking model and infrastructure metrics - Experience implementing data quality monitoring and feature drift detection in production environments

We are looking for an experienced MLOps Engineer to architect and operationalize scalable machine learning infrastructure on Azure within a decentralized data platform environment. Title: Data Science and AI Expert
Description:
Company Description

Sopra Steria offers tailored, end-to-end corporate technology and software solutions to help clients make bold choices and deliver results. Successfully so! With more than 56.000 colleagues in 30 countries, we rank as Europe’s leading digital solutions provider. Some of the most successful companies in Europe rely on our technology due to our commitment to innovation, collaboration, and value in business development.

The world is how we shape it. Let’s shape it together.

Job Description

Are you passionate about using data science and AI to real-world problems? At Sopra Steria, we’re looking for an experienced and versatile professional to help lead the design and delivery of advanced analytics and AI solutions across a wide range of domainssuch as public services, utilities, healthcare, mobility, and beyond.

In this role, you will work on impactful projects from ideation to delivery. You’ll collaborate closely with both technical and business stakeholders transforming challenges into actionable insights and production-ready solutions. Whether it’s designing an optimization algorithm, building an LLM-based assistant, or delivering a forecasting pipeline, you’ll help clients unlock value from data.

We’re looking for someone who combines deep technical expertise with the maturity to take ownership, work autonomously, and guide teams and clients through the ever-evolving AI landscape.

Qualifications

We’re seeking passionate colleagues who are eager to push the boundaries of digital transformation with AI. If you recognize yourself in most of the following, we’d love to talk:


You have at least 5 years of experience in delivering real-world AI and data science solutions in production environments.
You are proficient in Python and comfortable with frameworks like scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch, or similar.
You have practical experience with machine learning, and ideally exposure to domains like natural language processing, computer vision, predictive analytics, LLM’s or optimization.
You’ve worked with at least one major cloud provider (Azure, AWS, or GCP), and understand how to bring models into production securely and efficiently.
You understand how to balance model performance with business relevance, and how to translate analytical outcomes into actionable results.
You enjoy working in a team, guiding junior colleagues, and collaborating with clients to define solution architectures and technical roadmaps.
You’re comfortable interacting with stakeholders and can communicate complex concepts clearly and persuasively.
Experience in project ownership, technical leadership, or contributing to AI strategies.
You are fluent in English, and speak either Dutch or French.


What We Can Offer You


A key role in one of Europe’s leading digital transformation companies, where innovation meets real-world impact.
The opportunity to work on high-impact projects in AI and advanced analytics across industries and public institutions.
A culture of collaboration and continuous learning, supported by an expert network of consultants, architects, and engineers.
Access to our Sopra Steria Academy, with a wide range of training opportunities and certifications.
A hybrid and flexible working model, with access to offices across Belgium.
Career development opportunities both locally and internationally, within a growing Data & AI practice.


Additional Information

Our employees are known for making bold choices and delivering results. Work among high-level professionals at the forefront of corporate software solutions and innovation at Europe’s Leading Digital Service Provider.  

We offer a generous employee benefits package that includes:


A company car or mobility budget
Laptop, phone & phone subscription
A competitive salary and indefinite contract
Insurance coverage, meal vouchers, eco-cheques, and more
Continuous learning opportunities through the Sopra Steria Academy to support your career development


People are the cornerstone of our success. That’s why we aspire to be bolder together. Our goal is to build high-functioning teams and healthy team environments that inspire and help each other to deliver excellence for each of our customers.  

Excited about this job opportunity? Ready to shape the world with us? Great! We are looking forward to your application! 

______

Sopra Steria is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to age, ancestry, nationality, color, family or medical leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, gender (including pregnancy), sexual orientation or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. We foster a work environment that is inclusive and respectful of all differences.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Nexeo Belgium is a consulting company supporting clients in the banking, finance, insurance, and public sectors throughout their transformation projects.




We are currently looking for a Senior Data Scientist to join the Predictive Models Team within the Marketing and Communication department of a leading organization.

Mission Context

The Predictive Models Team provides responsible AI models to enable impactful and efficient campaigns by optimizing reach and relevance for customers and employees.

As a Senior Data Scientist, you will tackle various business challenges, build models to support customer journey-driven marketing campaigns, and collaborate closely with a team of motivated data scientists eager to share expertise and learn continuously.

Key Responsibilities

Capture business needs, determine feasibility, and design predictive models to identify the most relevant target groups
Build, deploy, and monitor responsible AI models, including using LLMs for text classification and summarization
Create pipelines to clean and structure web analytics data and detect customer intent
Apply best practices for AI model explainability, interpretability, and validation
Translate technical insights into actionable business recommendations for Marketing and Communication teams
Support campaign measurement, testing, and adoption of AI-driven solutions

Profile

Languages

English – Fluent
Dutch – Fluent OR French – Fluent

Education

Master’s degree or higher in a quantitative field (Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science) or equivalent experience

Experience & Technical Skills

Mandatory

5+ years of experience in data science and predictive modeling
Expert in Python and Python data science libraries (scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow)
Expertise in customer journey modeling (e.g., next best action)
Strong knowledge of ML algorithms, statistics, and experiment design
Expertise in LLMs for text classification, summarization, and responsible AI practices
SQL, git, bash

Preferred

Incremental learning, uplift modeling
Marketing Mix Modeling and optimization under constraints
SAS, Streamlit

Soft Skills

Take ownership and drive projects end-to-end
Translate data insights into actionable business decisions
Quick learner, autonomous, analytical, and problem-solving mindset
Team player with clear and concise communication
Proactive and results-oriented




This is a high-impact opportunity to work on AI-driven predictive models within marketing and communication, collaborating with a team of experts and influencing campaign outcomes with advanced analytics.

#DataScientist #AI #PredictiveModels #MachineLearning #MarketingAnalytics #ResponsibleAI #Nexeo #HybridWork #CareerOpportunity

Title: Machine Learning Engineer - Data Scientist.
Description:
Machine Learning Engineer - Data Scientist

Description Du Poste

Dans l'équipe AI&Analytics Solutions, nous voulons nous assurer que nous plaçons la prise de décision intelligente (augmentation humaine ou automatisée) au premier plan de nos processus/parcours en établissant une culture de données et d’expérimentation qui pourrait finalement devenir un avantage concurrentiel dans le monde en rapide évolution d’aujourd’hui.

Avez-vous une passion pour découvrir des insights à partir de données et les transformer en solutions impactantes ? Êtes-vous enthousiaste à l'idée de créer de la valeur commerciale à partir de données ?

En tant que Machine Learning Engineer - Data Scientist dans notre équipe, vous allez :


Travailler en étroite collaboration avec les parties prenantes pour comprendre et formuler leurs défis en tant que problèmes analytiques.
Appliquer le machine learning, les méthodes statistiques et les techniques d’optimisation pour développer des solutions innovantes qui impactent l'entreprise.
Concevoir, construire et évaluer des modèles prédictifs et prescriptifs, en assurant leur fiabilité et robustesse.
Diriger le cycle de vie complet des produits ML : de l'idéation et du prototypage à la mise en production et à l’évaluation continue.
Collaborer avec d’autres Data Scientists pour partager des connaissances, bonnes pratiques, et améliorer continuellement nos méthodologies.
Garantir la qualité et la performance de vos solutions par des tests rigoureux et une surveillance continue après déploiement.
Contribuer à notre communauté data science en développant des ressources réutilisables, en partageant votre expertise et en participant à des initiatives d’innovation.


Profil

En tant que Machine Learning Engineer - Data Scientist, vous avez :


Au moins 2 ans d’expérience en tant que Data Scientist/ML Engineer ou dans un rôle analytique similaire.
Un master dans un domaine pertinent (ex. Mathématiques appliquées, Statistiques, Data Science, Physique, Informatique).
Une solide connaissance pratique du machine learning, des statistiques et des techniques d’optimisation, avec une expérience concrète dans leur application à des problèmes réels. Expérience dans le développement, l’évaluation et le déploiement de solutions data science de bout en bout.
Maîtrise de Python et des bibliothèques data science courantes.
Familiarité avec les environnements de cloud computing.
Excellentes compétences en communication, capables d’expliquer des concepts complexes à des audiences techniques et non techniques.
Maîtrise de l’anglais, ainsi que du French ou Dutch.


Et vous êtes également :


Passionné par la résolution de problèmes d’affaires avec des données et de l’analytics.
Orienté résultats, capable de concilier excellence technique et besoins business.
Curieux et désireux d’apprendre et d’expérimenter continuellement avec de nouvelles techniques.
Collaboratif et capable de prospérer en équipe.
Proactif, analytique et orienté solutions.


AXA souhaite refléter la diversité de la société dans laquelle nous vivons et mène donc depuis des années une politique active en faveur de la diversité, de la non-discrimination et de l'égalité des chances. Dans ce cadre, nous garantissons un suivi égal et un accès équitable au recrutement, indépendamment de l'âge, de l'origine, du contexte culturel, du handicap, du genre, de l'orientation sexuelle ou d'autres caractéristiques.

AXA Belgium

Nous sommes plus de 3000 employés, travaillant dans nos bureaux modernes situés à Bruxelles, Anvers, Eupen et Liège. Nous servons 3 millions de clients en partenariat avec 3200 courtiers et nous sommes fiers d’être le premier assureur auto du pays. Notre ambition est de ne pas seulement être un payeur, mais plutôt un véritable partenaire pour nos clients.

Nos quatre valeurs principales nous poussent chaque jour à donner le meilleur de nous-mêmes pour nos clients, tout en collaborant efficacement avec nos collègues: Customer First, Integrity, Courage et One AXA

Chez AXA, nous rejetons toute forme de discrimination déloyale ou illégale. Plus d’informations sur notre  politique de Diversité et d’Inclusion.

Ce que nous vous offrons

Une rémunération et des avantages en lien avec votre travail

Vous bénéficierez d’un salaire compétitif, complété éventuellement d’un bonus discrétionnaire lié au résultat personnel avec des chèques-repas, des écochèques, du remboursement total des transports en commun et un plan cafétaria.

Tous les avantages d’un assureur comme employeur

Vous bénéficierez d’une assurance pension et d’une assurance hospitalisation et soins ambulatoires (également à un tarif préférentiel pour les membres de votre ménage) ainsi que d’une réduction allant jusqu'à 30 % sur nos autres produits d'assurance.

Un emploi qui s'adapte à votre vie et à vos rêves

Un horaire flexible de 35 heures par semaine, 36 jours de congé par an pour vous ressourcer, ainsi que la possibilité de travailler partiellement depuis la maison.

Vous bénéficierez de cours de sport au travail, de programmes de bien-être pour les employés et de formations, afin de vous maintenir en pleine forme, tant physiquement que mentalement.

Chez AXA, nous rejetons toute forme de discrimination déloyale ou illégale. Plus d’informations sur notre  politique de Diversité et d’Inclusion.

Ce que nous vous offrons

Une rémunération et des avantages en lien avec votre travail

Vous bénéficierez d’un salaire compétitif, complété éventuellement d’un bonus discrétionnaire lié au résultat personnel avec des chèques-repas, des écochèques, du remboursement total des transports en commun et un plan cafétaria.

Tous les avantages d’un assureur comme employeur

Vous bénéficierez d’une assurance pension et d’une assurance hospitalisation et soins ambulatoires (également à un tarif préférentiel pour les membres de votre ménage) ainsi que d’une réduction allant jusqu'à 30 % sur nos autres produits d'assurance.

Un emploi qui s'adapte à votre vie et à vos rêves

Un horaire flexible de 35 heures par semaine, 36 jours de congé par an pour vous ressourcer, ainsi que la possibilité de travailler partiellement depuis la maison.

Vous bénéficierez de cours de sport au travail, de programmes de bien-être pour les employés et de formations, afin de vous maintenir en pleine forme, tant physiquement que mentalement.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
We are seeking a Data Scientist with strong analytical and problem-solving expertise who can transform complex datasets into clear, actionable insights that drive business growth and performance. You’ll work closely with our Strategic Leads to uncover performance patterns, validate hypotheses, and find the levers that drive real impact. This role reports directly to the Head of Growth and will play an integral role in advancing data-driven decision-making across WLDM’s clients.

What You’ll Do


Develop data models to forecast performance outcomes and ROI
Automate analysis of key metrics, trends, and content performance
Identify patterns, correlations and opportunities within large, messy datasets
Build and maintain dashboards that enable real-time insights and data driven decision making
Run statistical tests to validate hypotheses and measure experimental outcomes
Deliver clear, actionable insights from data sources that bridge data science with strategic execution


What We’re Looking For

Required


2+ years of experience working with business, behavioral, or performance datasets
Advanced proficiency in Python, SQL, and data visualization tools (Looker, Power BI, or similar)
Experience with APIs, web scraping, and NLP or text-based data analysis
Strong ability to to distill complex technical findings into clear, strategic insights


Preferred


Familiarity with clustering, graph-based modeling, or knowledge extraction from text data
Experience applying Machine Learning or statistical methods to optimize business performance


What We Offer


Fully remote, async-first team — work from anywhere
High autonomy and ownership — no micromanagement, just results
Work alongside private equity firms and 8-figure global brands
Defined growth paths into senior data strategy, analytics, or innovation roles


Working Hours


Fully remote position
Flexible schedule, async-first collaboration
Salary starting from $2500 USD per month

Title: Senior Data Scientist - GenAI
Description:
Join Artefact Belgium: Lead the Future of Generative AI and Large Language Models

Are you a seasoned AI professional with a passion for generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)? Artefact Belgium is looking for a Senior Data Scientist - Gen AI to drive innovation and lead cutting-edge projects. If you have at least four years of experience in AI and consulting, combined with a talent for project and team management, we invite you to shape the future of LLM-driven solutions with us in Brussels.

Who You Are


Experienced professional: At least 4 years of experience in AI, with a strong background in consulting and delivering impactful projects for diverse clients.
Leader and collaborator: Proven experience managing teams, guiding junior colleagues, and fostering collaboration in multidisciplinary environments.
Innovative thinker: Able to identify emerging trends in generative AI and apply them to real-world challenges.
Results-oriented: Comfortable handling end-to-end project delivery, from ideation and development to deployment and client engagement.
Eager to grow: Passionate about advancing your expertise in LLMs and generative AI while mentoring others.


Who We Are

Artefact is a leading Data & AI consulting firm at the forefront of AI innovation. With a global presence, we collaborate with top-tier clients across industries, including healthcare, manufacturing, and media, to harness the transformative power of generative AI. Our team in Belgium is specialized in LLM and GenAI projects, working on the most advanced applications of these technologies. Our inclusive and forward-thinking culture encourages creativity, collaboration, and professional growth.

What You Will Do


Lead and deliver LLM projects: Manage the design, development, and deployment of advanced LLM-powered solutions, including RAG systems, agentic AI architectures, and multi-modal applications for our clients.
Drive technical excellence: Architect and deploy advanced AI systems leveraging cutting-edge techniques such as parameter-efficient fine-tuning (LoRA/QLoRA), prompt orchestration, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), function/tool calling, multi-agent workflows, and emerging interoperability standards like the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
Establish evaluation frameworks: Design and implement comprehensive quality metrics and benchmarks to ensure production-ready, reliable AI solutions that meet client requirements.
Mentor and manage teams: Guide junior engineers and data scientists in implementing cutting-edge AI technologies, fostering best practices in LLM development and deployment.
Innovate and strategize: Drive experimentation with state-of-the-art LLMs to identify new use cases, optimize performance through techniques like quantization and inference optimization, and develop generative AI tools and products.
Build scalable infrastructure: Oversee the deployment and maintenance of AI systems, including model serving, API development, vector databases, orchestration frameworks (LangChain, LlamaIndex), and cloud infrastructure.
Engage with clients: Collaborate with client teams to translate their business needs into impactful AI solutions, providing technical leadership and strategic guidance throughout project lifecycles.
Shape our expertise: Contribute to the company's thought leadership by publishing insights, presenting at conferences, or driving internal training on emerging LLM technologies and best practices.


What You Bring


A degree in computer science, engineering, mathematics, or a related field; advanced degrees (MSc or PhD) are a plus.
At least 4 years of professional experience in AI, with a strong focus on Data, Machine Learning, LLMs, or generative AI.
Consulting experience, including managing client relationships and delivering tailored solutions.
Deep expertise in modern LLM techniques including fine-tuning, RAG systems, agentic AI, evaluation frameworks, and production deployment.
Strong programming skills in Python and extensive experience with NLP/AI frameworks (e.g., Hugging Face, OpenAI, Anthropic, LangGraph, Agno, ….).
Leadership and project management skills, with a proven track record of managing teams and delivering complex AI projects.
Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills. Proficiency in English, Dutch, or French is required; additional languages are a plus.


What We Offer


Competitive compensation aligned with your expertise and experience.
High-impact opportunities to work with state-of-the-art LLM technologies and solve real-world challenges.
A leadership role in a dynamic, collaborative, and innovative work environment.
Professional growth through dedicated resources for training, conferences, and thought leadership.
A chance to shape the direction of a fast-growing AI team within a global consulting firm.
A collaborative, inclusive workplace with access to global expertise through Artefact's network.


This position is based in Brussels, with opportunities to collaborate on projects worldwide.

At Artefact Belgium, we value expertise, leadership, and a drive to innovate. If you're ready to lead and make a tangible impact in the world of generative AI, we'd love to hear from you
Show more

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
We are seeking a skilled Machine Learning Engineer to design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning models within a cloud-based environment. This role will focus on developing marketing-driven models, requiring a strong blend of technical expertise and an understanding of marketing use cases.




Key Responsibilities

Design, develop, and deploy machine learning models to support marketing initiatives.
Work with large-scale datasets using Google Cloud Platform (GCP), particularly BigQuery.
Collaborate with marketing and data teams to translate business needs into data-driven solutions.
Build and maintain robust data pipelines for model training and evaluation.
Optimize model performance and ensure scalability in production environments.
Write clean, efficient, and maintainable Python code.
Monitor and improve existing models based on performance metrics and business impact.




Required Skills & Experience

Proven experience as a Machine Learning Engineer or similar role.
Strong proficiency in Python for data science and machine learning applications.
Hands-on experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP).
Solid experience working with BigQuery for data analysis and processing.
Understanding of machine learning frameworks and libraries (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn).
Experience building and deploying models in production environments.
Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.




Preferred Qualifications

Experience working with marketing data or building marketing-focused models (e.g., customer segmentation, churn prediction, campaign optimization).
Familiarity with data visualization tools and techniques.
Knowledge of MLOps best practices.
Experience collaborating with cross-functional teams, especially marketing stakeholders.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
ML OPS Engineer / AI Engineer

About NORRIQ

NORRIQ is an international group based in Denmark and Belgium, comprising over 350 consultants dedicated to unlocking the full business potential of companies through technology and digital transformation.

We have a specialized Financial Services practice that is experiencing rapid growth. Over 100 consultants focus exclusively on the financial services sector, assisting banks and insurances in redesigning their operations to meet changing client expectations, adopt new technologies, and adhere to evolving regulations (DORA, RIS, AI Act, AML, KYC, etc).

At NORRIQ, our employees are at the heart of our organization and our success.. We hire and nurture passionate individuals who strive for excellence for both our clients and colleagues. Our culture supports personal and professional growth with training, opportunities, and creative freedom. Regardless of when you join or how long you stay, you'll experience an unforgettable NORRIQ journey and become a part of our extended family. Connected forever!

Role Overview

As an MLOps Engineer / AI Engineer, you will play a central role in bridging Data Science and IT Operations. You will design, deploy, and scale production-grade machine learning and AI solutions for our financial services clients. Your mission is to ensure robust, secure, and efficient AI operations—covering deployment, observability, lifecycle management, and continuous improvement.

This role is ideal for someone who loves building AI infrastructure, optimizing performance, and enabling data scientists to move faster and safer.

Key Responsibilities


Deploy ML/AI Models: Work with Data Scientists to deploy secure, scalable, and reliable models into production.
Build CI/CD Pipelines: Automate testing, packaging, and deployment for data and model workflows.
Manage Data Pipelines: Design and maintain efficient data flows across storage systems, databases, and platforms.
Set Up Environments: Manage dependencies, development environments, and tooling for smooth project onboarding.
Operate Kubernetes: Deploy and maintain containerized AI components using Kubernetes.
Monitor & Troubleshoot: Oversee model/application performance, resolve incidents, and conduct root-cause analyses.
Optimize & Innovate: Identify improvements, adopt new technologies, and enhance AI engineering practices.


Requirements

Education


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
Strong background in programming, system engineering, or DevOps is highly valued.


Technical Skills


Strong proficiency in Python.
Experience with REST APIs and Git.
Hands-on expertise in CI/CD and MLOps/DevOps practices.
Practical experience with Docker and Kubernetes.
Familiarity with Linux, software testing, and modern software engineering workflows.
Nice to have: SQL/PostgreSQL, MLflow, Helm, or related tooling.


Soft Skills


Collaborative and team-oriented mindset.
Proactive, solution-driven, and comfortable working in dynamic environments.
Strong communication, analytical, and problem-solving abilities.


Languages


Fluent in English (spoken and written).
Knowledge of additional languages (Dutch or French) is a plus.


Why Joining Us?


You will be part of a fast growing international company with a unique people culture
A very competitive salary package including a company car, iPhone and different allowances
You will get extensive trainings in Belgium and Denmark on technical matters, as well as soft skills and project management, and you will have access to new technologies and innovative equipment.
You will join a dynamic and growing team with a great mix of young and experienced professionals focusing on financial services.
Show more

Title: Data Scientist - Consulting & AI Delivery
Description:
Are you excited by solving real business problems with data, analytics, and AI?

Do you enjoy combining structured analysis, client interaction, and modern tools to create measurable impact?

Are you curious about new ways of working, including AI-assisted and agentic workflows, while staying pragmatic and quality-driven?

At Agilytic, we help clients turn business objectives into measurable results through smarter use of data. We are looking for a colleague who is comfortable moving between business understanding, quantitative analysis, and hands-on delivery.

What is your mission?

Help innovative companies turn their objectives into concrete results through smarter use of data, analytics, automation, and AI.

You will work at the intersection of business and technology to help clients make better decisions, improve performance, and implement solutions that create tangible value.

What will you do day to day?

As a Data Scientist (Consulting & AI Delivery), you will contribute to the end-to-end delivery of our projects, from problem framing to implementation and client adoption.

You will work closely with client stakeholders and internal team members to:


Translate business questions into analytical approaches and decision-support solutions
Structure and prioritize requirements with clients
Collect, clean, transform, and validate data from multiple sources
Perform descriptive, diagnostic, and predictive analyses where relevant
Build dashboards, models, reports, and decision-support tools
Prototype and automate parts of the analysis or delivery workflow
Communicate findings, trade-offs, and recommendations clearly to technical and non-technical audiences
Support implementation so that analysis leads to concrete action
Use modern AI-assisted tools responsibly to accelerate research, coding, testing, documentation, and delivery


Our Assignments Typically Include


Data audit
Data preparation
Descriptive and diagnostic analysis
Modeling where relevant
Dashboarding and reporting
Presenting results and recommendations
Supporting implementation and business adoption


Senior Data Scientists also supervise and coach junior team members in both client delivery and internal initiatives. This guidance helps them quickly develop the business, consulting, and technical skills needed to manage projects independently.

How We Work

We are pragmatic and outcome-driven. We use the right level of sophistication for the problem at hand.

That means we do not apply advanced techniques for their own sake. Sometimes the right answer is a predictive model, an automation, or a more advanced analytical workflow. Sometimes it is a clear dashboard, a robust dataset, or a simple analysis that helps a client act quickly and confidently.

Increasingly, this also includes AI-assisted and agentic ways of working. We welcome candidates who are already comfortable using tools such as Claude Code, Codex, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, or similar tools to accelerate research, coding, testing, documentation, and delivery.

What matters most is not tool usage alone, but professional judgment: being able to frame the problem correctly, validate outputs carefully, communicate clearly, and maintain quality, security, and maintainability.

Requirements

Do you recognize yourself?Your background


You hold a master’s degree in a quantitative, scientific, engineering, or business-related field such as Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, Physics, Biology, Computer Science, or similar
You are fluent in English and in either Dutch or French


Your core skills


You have a strong foundation in analytics, statistics, and structured problem solving
You have hands-on experience with Python and SQL for data preparation, analysis, and modeling
You are comfortable working with real-world datasets and transforming ambiguous business questions into structured analytical work
You can communicate insights clearly and adapt your message to both technical and non-technical audiences
You are comfortable working directly with clients and presenting recommendations


Nice to have


Experience with ETL / ELT workflows, cloud data environments, APIs, or analytics engineering
Experience with BI and reporting tools
Familiarity with machine learning, forecasting, segmentation, optimization, or experimentation
Experience with version control, testing, and collaborative development practices
Familiarity with AI-assisted or agentic coding workflows and tools such as Claude Code, Codex, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, or similar


We will be especially interested in candidates who can show how they use modern tools to work faster and better while maintaining strong standards of rigor, validation, and documentation.

Your personal style


You have strong analytical skills and can collect, structure, analyze, and interpret data in service of a clear business objective
You have a problem-solving mindset and take a pragmatic approach to data science
You look for the simplest solution that creates real value
You are proactive, autonomous, and comfortable taking initiative
You care about work quality and bring a professional attitude to client service
You are curious about new techniques and tools and keep learning
You combine business understanding with enough technical depth to deliver credible, useful work


Must have

You are eligible to work in Belgium and the European Union Please note that we do not offer relocation support

You will be a great fit if


You want to help shape a growing organization and grow with it
You enjoy combining business understanding with hands-on analytical work
You can move from ambiguity to structured action
You are comfortable interacting with stakeholders at all levels, from operational teams to executive leadership
You are curious about better ways of working, including AI-assisted delivery
You care about clarity, quality, and measurable client impact


Some signs this may not be the right fit


You do not enjoy interacting with clients or presenting your work from time to time All our Data Scientists are client-facing
You are not comfortable working at the client’s premises on average 2 to 3 days a week and from our offices in Brussels on average 1 to 2 days a week
You are looking for a purely technical role with little business interaction
You are not comfortable working in a pragmatic consulting environment where the best solution is not always the most technically sophisticated one
You do not have an entrepreneurial mindset and are not motivated to go the extra mile to deliver quality work or contribute to the growth of the organization


Benefits

Working at Agilytic

We aim to create a work environment that allows you to do exceptional work, grow your skills, and enjoy the journey while doing it.

You may work on assignments anywhere in Belgium and neighboring countries such as Luxembourg, France, the Netherlands, and the UK. You can expect to interact with both IT/data specialists and business decision-makers across areas such as marketing, process improvement, and customer care.

We believe that data is a means to an end, not an end in itself. We do not use advanced techniques when they are not suited to the problem we need to solve. For example, we will not build a complex machine learning model if a well-structured dashboard or a focused analysis answers the client’s question more effectively. In practice, a significant share of our missions do not require machine learning at all.

This is a role for someone who wants to combine analytical depth, business relevance, and modern ways of working in a consulting environment.

Salary And Benefits


Competitive remuneration package: We offer a competitive remuneration package, including a cafeteria plan in which you can allocate your variable remuneration to the advantages that suit you best, such as extra days off, electronics, or cash.
Training roadmap: We will define a training roadmap together, taking into account both our internal capabilities and your personal interests.
Supportive structure and transparency: What we do not yet have in scale, we make up for with flexibility, support, and transparency. You can expect individual coaching and a clear approach to career development.
Modern tools: Tools are a means to an end. We want them to help you do your best work, not slow you down. You can expect up-to-date hardware and software, including modern AI-assisted tools where useful.
A serious job that does not take itself too seriously:
We care deeply about the quality of our work, but we also like working with people who can keep things human.


Application

If this sounds like the kind of role in which you would thrive, we would like to hear from you.

In your application, feel free to share examples of analyses, dashboards, code, automations, or AI-assisted workflows you have built. We are particularly interested in how you think, how you validate your work, and how you create business value.
Show more
