  • Prototype and automate parts of the analysis or delivery workflow
  • Communicate findings, trade-offs, and recommendations clearly to technical and non-technical audiences
  • Support implementation so that analysis leads to concrete action
  • Use modern AI-assisted tools responsibly to accelerate research, coding, testing, documentation, and delivery

Our Assignments Typically Include

  • Data audit
  • Data preparation
  • Descriptive and diagnostic analysis
  • Modeling where relevant
  • Dashboarding and reporting
  • Presenting results and recommendations
  • Supporting implementation and business adoption

Senior Data Scientists also supervise and coach junior team members in both client delivery and internal initiatives. This guidance helps them quickly develop the business, consulting, and technical skills needed to manage projects independently.

How We Work

We are pragmatic and outcome-driven. We use the right level of sophistication for the problem at hand.

That means we do not apply advanced techniques for their own sake. Sometimes the right answer is a predictive model, an automation, or a more advanced analytical workflow. Sometimes it is a clear dashboard, a robust dataset, or a simple analysis that helps a client act quickly and confidently.

Increasingly, this also includes AI-assisted and agentic ways of working. We welcome candidates who are already comfortable using tools such as Claude Code, Codex, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, or similar tools to accelerate research, coding, testing, documentation, and delivery.

What matters most is not tool usage alone, but professional judgment: being able to frame the problem correctly, validate outputs carefully, communicate clearly, and maintain quality, security, and maintainability.

Requirements

Do you recognize yourself?Your background

  • You hold a master’s degree in a quantitative, scientific, engineering, or business-related field such as Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, Physics, Biology, Computer Science, or similar
  • You are fluent in English and in either Dutch or French

Your core skills

  • You have a strong foundation in analytics, statistics, and structured problem solving
  • You have hands-on experience with Python and SQL for data preparation, analysis, and modeling
  • You are comfortable working with real-world datasets and transforming ambiguous business questions into structured analytical work
  • You can communicate insights clearly and adapt your message to both technical and non-technical audiences
  • You are comfortable working directly with clients and presenting recommendations

Nice to have

  • Experience with ETL / ELT workflows, cloud data environments, APIs, or analytics engineering
  • Experience with BI and reporting tools
  • Familiarity with machine learning, forecasting, segmentation, optimization, or experimentation
  • Experience with version control, testing, and collaborative development practices
  • Familiarity with AI-assisted or agentic coding workflows and tools such as Claude Code, Codex, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, or similar

We will be especially interested in candidates who can show how they use modern tools to work faster and better while maintaining strong standards of rigor, validation, and documentation.

Your personal style

  • You have strong analytical skills and can collect, structure, analyze, and interpret data in service of a clear business objective
  • You have a problem-solving mindset and take a pragmatic approach to data science
  • You look for the simplest solution that creates real value
  • You are proactive, autonomous, and comfortable taking initiative
  • You care about work quality and bring a professional attitude to client service
  • You are curious about new techniques and tools and keep learning
  • You combine business understanding with enough technical depth to deliver credible, useful work

Must have

You are eligible to work in Belgium and the European Union Please note that we do not offer relocation support

You will be a great fit if

  • You want to help shape a growing organization and grow with it
  • You enjoy combining business understanding with hands-on analytical work
  • You can move from ambiguity to structured action
  • You are comfortable interacting with stakeholders at all levels, from operational teams to executive leadership
  • You are curious about better ways of working, including AI-assisted delivery
  • You care about clarity, quality, and measurable client impact

Some signs this may not be the right fit

  • You do not enjoy interacting with clients or presenting your work from time to time All our Data Scientists are client-facing
  • You are not comfortable working at the client’s premises on average 2 to 3 days a week and from our offices in Brussels on average 1 to 2 days a week
  • You are looking for a purely technical role with little business interaction
  • You are not comfortable working in a pragmatic consulting environment where the best solution is not always the most technically sophisticated one
  • You do not have an entrepreneurial mindset and are not motivated to go the extra mile to deliver quality work or contribute to the growth of the organization

Benefits

Working at Agilytic

We aim to create a work environment that allows you to do exceptional work, grow your skills, and enjoy the journey while doing it.

You may work on assignments anywhere in Belgium and neighboring countries such as Luxembourg, France, the Netherlands, and the UK. You can expect to interact with both IT/data specialists and business decision-makers across areas such as marketing, process improvement, and customer care.

We believe that data is a means to an end, not an end in itself. We do not use advanced techniques when they are not suited to the problem we need to solve. For example, we will not build a complex machine learning model if a well-structured dashboard or a focused analysis answers the client’s question more effectively. In practice, a significant share of our missions do not require machine learning at all.

This is a role for someone who wants to combine analytical depth, business relevance, and modern ways of working in a consulting environment.

Salary And Benefits

  • Competitive remuneration package: We offer a competitive remuneration package, including a cafeteria plan in which you can allocate your variable remuneration to the advantages that suit you best, such as extra days off, electronics, or cash.
  • Training roadmap: We will define a training roadmap together, taking into account both our internal capabilities and your personal interests.
  • Supportive structure and transparency: What we do not yet have in scale, we make up for with flexibility, support, and transparency. You can expect individual coaching and a clear approach to career development.
  • Modern tools: Tools are a means to an end. We want them to help you do your best work, not slow you down. You can expect up-to-date hardware and software, including modern AI-assisted tools where useful.
  • A serious job that does not take itself too seriously:
  • We care deeply about the quality of our work, but we also like working with people who can keep things human.

Application

If this sounds like the kind of role in which you would thrive, we would like to hear from you.

In your application, feel free to share examples of analyses, dashboards, code, automations, or AI-assisted workflows you have built. We are particularly interested in how you think, how you validate your work, and how you create business value.

Title: Junior Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Do you want to kickstart your career in Data & AI? Then you’ll definitely be interested in our training program for young graduates!

Please note that candidates hired for this position will start in September 2026.




🤖 The Job

At Dataroots, we combine our consulting approach with strong technical skills to build machine learning models that help businesses improve how they operate. As a Junior Machine Learning Engineer, you’ll join our team of specialists (including Data Engineers, Cloud Architects, and Data Strategists) working together to design, build, and implement tailored, high quality AI-powered solutions for our clients.




We encourage learning and growth! You'll have chances to learn from and mentor other teammates, share what you know, and keep up-to-date with the latest industry trends through our internal training and development programs.

If you're excited about data, enjoy exploring new ideas, and want to create machine learning models that make a real difference, you'll fit right in!




🎉 What you'll find at Dataroots

Since 2016, Dataroots has been committing its excellent consulting services to help clients excel in their data-driven operations.

Our approach focuses on team well-being, fostering diversity, and promoting knowledge sharing. By focusing on these values, we've achieved healthy and sustainable growth over the years. Through our services, we actively encourage professional growth by exposing our employees to different industries, tools, technologies,... broadening their scope in the process.

As part of the Talan Group, we are able to take this approach to the next level, adding the element of international mobility to our employees’ career journey.

Join a team where your work will have a direct impact on both our clients and our journey. Learn more about our DNA at [https://dataroots.io/our-dna].




💼 What we’re looking for

You're strongly motivated to become an excellent Machine Learning Engineer.
Solving problems is what drives you and you see Machine Learning as a tool to achieve that, rather than the end goal.
You have a solid foundation in Machine Learning theory and have trained and/or deployed your first AI models.
You are eager to build and experiment with (gen)AI-driven solutions. You have a foundational understanding of genAI concepts and are excited to turn AI potential into functional, value-adding tools.
Programming in Python is something you’re very comfortable with.
Cloud platforms like AWS, GCP or Azure are not entirely new to you. You’ve been in contact with at least one.
You have heard of tools like Apache Spark and Databricks and understand their use in the data landscape.
To you, CI/CD or MLOps don’t sound like Star Wars characters. You are interested in learning about data architecture and infrastructure.
Picking up on new technologies and frameworks comes naturally to you and doesn’t scare you.
Helping clients in overcoming their challenges energises you. You have strong communication skills and like putting them to good use.
A first practical experience is considered a big plus (internship, personal project, first professional experience,…)
You speak Dutch & English fluently, knowledge of French is a plus.




This list was probably a bit much. Don't worry, we don't expect you to tick every box from the beginning. Most of all: we are looking for colleagues with the same passion for personal and team development as the rest of us 🤝




💰 The Offer

+ An attractive salary with extralegal benefits, including:

Mobility budget or a company car with fuel/charging card
Hospitalization and group insurance
High-end laptop
Smartphone with subscription
Substantial amount of holidays
Meal vouchers
...

+ Diverse and welcoming work environment, where you’ll collaborate and unwind with colleagues from different cultures and disciplines. The organization of both fun & professional events and initiatives is actively encouraged and supported.

+ Opportunities for individual training and team learning.

+ Tons of team-building events and sports initiatives to stay connected and unwind.




📍 Where you’ll work

Our main offices are in Leuven and Ghent. These spaces are perfect for both focusing and unwinding with colleagues, as well as brainstorming sessions and team events. While much of your work can be performed remotely, you will also work on-site with clients.




🎯 Ready to join Dataroots?

Excited to shape the future of AI as a Machine Learning Engineer with us? We’re looking forward to hearing from you! Apply now and start a new chapter with Dataroots! 🎉

Title: Freelance Machine Learning Engineer (Azure / PySpark)
Description:
Willing to start a new freelance challenge? Get in touch.




What You'll Do

Design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning models for predictive use cases
Process and analyze large datasets using distributed computing (PySpark)
Develop and optimize data pipelines in collaboration with data engineers
Translate business needs into robust data science solutions
Perform feature engineering, model evaluation, and validation
Deploy models into production and contribute to MLOps practices
Work within Azure-based data platforms
Continuously improve ML workflows, tools, and best practices

Solid understanding of statistics and data analysis
Experience with Azure data ecosystem
Strong experience with PySpark / distributed data processing
Proven experience handling large datasets and building pipelines
Experience with model deployment and production environments
Ability to collaborate with technical and business stakeholders

Title: Machine Learning Engineer - Data Scientist
Description:
Functiebeschrijving

In het AI&Analytics Solutions team willen we ervoor zorgen dat we slimme besluitvorming (menselijke augmentatie of geautomatiseerd) naar de voorgrond brengen van onze processen/journeys door het opbouwen van een datas en experimentatiecultuur die uiteindelijk een concurrentievoordeel kan worden in de snel veranderende wereld van vandaag.

Heb je een passie voor het ontdekken van inzichten uit data en het omzetten hiervan in impactvolle oplossingen? Ben je enthousiast om zakelijke waarde uit data te halen?

Als Machine Learning Engineer - Data Scientist in ons team, zal je:


Nauw samenwerken met bedrijfsstakeholders om hun uitdagingen te begrijpen en te vertalen naar analytische problemen.
Machine learning, statistische methoden en optimalisatietechnieken toepassen om innovatieve oplossingen te ontwikkelen die zakelijke impact hebben.
Voorspellende en prescriptieve modellen ontwerpen, bouwen en evalueren, en zorgen voor betrouwbaarheid en robuustheid.
Leiding nemen in de volledige lifecycle van ML-producten: van idee en prototyping tot implementatie en voortdurende evaluatie in productie.
Samenwerken met andere Data Scientists om kennis, best practices te delen en onze methodologieën continu te verbeteren.
Zorgen voor de kwaliteit en prestaties van je oplossingen door rigoureus testen en voortdurende monitoring na deployment.
Bijdragen aan onze data science community door herbruikbare assets te ontwikkelen, je expertise te delen en deel te nemen aan innovatie-initiatieven.


Profiel

Als Machine Learning Engineer - Data Scientist heb je:


Minimaal 2 jaar ervaring als Data Scientist/ML Engineer of een vergelijkbare analytische rol.
Een masterdiploma in een relevant vakgebied (bijv. Toegepaste Wiskunde, Statistiek, Data Science, Natuurkunde, Informatica).
Sterke praktische kennis van machine learning, statistiek en optimalisatietechnieken, met hands-on ervaring in het toepassen ervan op echte problemen. Ervaring met het ontwikkelen, evalueren en implementeren van end-to-end data science oplossingen.
Vaardigheid in Python en gangbare data science libraries
Bekendheid met cloud computing omgevingen.
Uitstekende communicatieve vaardigheden, met het vermogen om complexe concepten uit te leggen aan zowel technische als niet-technische doelgroepen.
Vloeiend in Engels, evenals in French of Dutch


En je bent ook:


Gepassioneerd over het oplossen van zakelijke problemen met data en analytics.
Resultaatgericht, in staat om technische excellentie te combineren met zakelijke behoeften.
Nieuwsgierig en enthousiast om continu te leren en te experimenteren met nieuwe technieken.
Samenwerkingsgericht en gedijt in teamomgevingen.
Proactief, analytisch en oplossingsgericht.


AXA wil een weerspiegeling zijn van de diverse samenleving waarin wij leven en voert daarom al jaren een beleid van actieve diversiteit, non-discriminatie en gelijke kansen. In dit kader garanderen wij een gelijke behandeling en toegang tot selecties, ongeacht leeftijd, afkomst, culturele achtergrond, handicap, geslacht of seksuele geaardheid of andere kenmerken. Bij AXA wijzen wij alle vormen van oneerlijke of onwettige discriminatie af.

AXA Belgium

Meer dan 3000 mensen werken bij ons, vanuit onze vooruitstrevende kantoren in Brussel, Antwerpen, Eupen en Luik. We werken samen met 3200 makelaars. We hebben 3 miljoen klanten en zijn nummer 1 in autoverzekeringen. Het is onze ambitie om onze relatie met al onze klanten te transformeren van betaler naar partner om ons echt te onderscheiden.

Onze fundamenten

Onze belangrijkste leidraad zijn onze waarden: Customer first, Integrity, Courage en One AXA. Alles wat we doen, daar staan we als één team achter.

Bij AXA wijzen wij alle vormen van oneerlijke of onwettige discriminatie af. Meer info in ons Diversiteits- en Inclusiebeleid.

Wat we aanbieden

Een mooie beloning voor je werk

Een competitief loon, eventueel aangevuld met discretionaire persoonlijke en natuurlijk ook maaltijd- en ecocheques, een volledige terugbetaling van het openbaar vervoer en een cafetariaplan.

Alle voordelen van werken bij een verzekeraar

Van groepsverzekering tot ambulante en hospitalisatieverzekering (ook gezinsleden kunnen daarbij aansluiten aan een voordelig tarief) en met tot 30% korting op onze andere verzekeringsproducten. 

Een job die zich aanpast aan je leven en dromen

Een flexibel rooster van 35 uur per week, 36 dagen verlof per jaar om op te laden, evenals de mogelijkheid om gedeeltelijk van thuis uit te werken.

Wij bieden sportlessen op het werk, welzijnsprogramma's voor werknemers en trainingen, om u in topvorm te houden, zowel fysiek als mentaal.

Title: Data Engineer GCP – Full Remote
Description:
The Role




Portuguese client - We’re looking for a freelance GCP Data Engineer to design and define a company’s data architecture from scratch in a long-term, strategic project. You’ll work closely with engineering, data and business teams to shape data models, data flows, storage layers and governance, ensuring scalable, secure and efficient solutions.




Requirements

Strong experience in data engineer and data modeling
Hands-on experience with ETL/ELT processes and data integration
Knowledge of data governance, security and privacy
Experience with data warehouses and data lakes
Cloud data platforms: +5 years in GCP




Nice to Have

Big Data frameworks (Spark, Hadoop)
NoSQL databases (MongoDB, Cassandra)
Cloud certifications




Why This Project

End-to-end ownership of data architecture
Long-term, stable project
Real impact on technical strategy
Full remote with optional hybrid in Lisbon or Porto

Title: Lead Data Scientist
Description:
Roles & Responsibilities

Help shape the future of data science at KPMG Lighthouse

In a world where digital experiences are constantly evolving, data scientists play a key role in driving innovation. At KPMG Lighthouse, we’re looking for talent who not only turn data into smart solutions, but who can also inspire others with the art of the possible.




What you’ll do:

Drive the growth of Advanced Analytics and Machine Learning as a capability and service offering
Contribute to expanding our project portfolio across industries
Mentor and support junior team members
Engage with clients to understand business challenges and translate them into AI-driven solutions
Gather, structure, and analyze data using the most suitable analytical techniques
Communicate insights clearly to clients, highlighting the impact and value of your models
Work with IT specialists to bring your models into production
Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, stakeholders, and internal teams




Skills & Qualifications

The tasks of a data scientist manager are diverse, and we are not looking for the unicorn data scientist, but a mindset focused on learning on the job is vital!

Elements we hope to see in our data scientist managers:

Concrete experience delivering concrete AI products.
A master degree with strong elements of modern data science techniques: Machine Learning algorithms, coding, statistics,…
Other types of eduction are also valued.
A sound knowledge of algorithms, and the ability to critically apply this knowledge to practical problems.
Knowledge of common data science software. We have a Python-first setup, but will if needed tackle problems using R, SAS, SQL, Spark, and work with tools such as Jupyter Notebooks, IDE’s, Git, Microsoft Azure.
You confidently navigate AI topics and are able to communicate complex concepts to stakeholders, make ethically sound decisions and adapt to evolving technologies.
Obviously, additional bonus points for PhD’s, and more specific knowledge like e.g. Keras, D3.js
A positive, collaborative, and flexible mindset
Fluent professional proficiency in Dutch and English is required. (French is a plus)




We offer

As a certified Top Employer we offer:

A reward package that goes beyond salary. Think premium electric company car (BMW, Mercedes, Tesla, etc.) + charging card, net allowances, bonus, your choice of Apple/Android device, and lots of extras you can personalize with our cafeteria plan.
Peace of mind with full insurance coverage. We’ve got you covered with group and hospitalization insurance, plus optional extras like dental, eye care, and medical consultations.
Work in your own way. Enjoy flexible hours, hybrid working, and even the chance to work from anywhere in the world for 20 days a year.
We’re an international community that thrives on trust and collaboration. With inclusive communities for gender, LGBTQI+, internationals, and many more, you’ll truly feel like you can come as you are.
Your growth = our priority. You’ll get training and development tailored to your job, level and even personal goals, plus support from your personal buddy and performance manager.
More than colleagues. From sport challenges (Antwerp 10 Miles, Brussels Marathon, 1000km for KOTK) to volunteering (river clean-ups, tree planting), we make impact together through our Together@KPMG & KPMG Foundation programs.
We care about you. With initiatives such as confidential counselors, an employee assistance program, hotlines and more, we will support your emotional and mental health throughout your career.

Title: Expert in AI/ NLP/ ML
Description:
Description of the Tasks


Following tasks will be performed by the external service provider:

Design the IT architecture for solutions in the NLP / ML / AI fields, and implement PoCs such as in the ChatBot / RAG field;

Analyse data assets & business processes;

Analyse risk-level of existing LLMs implemented in few of EMPL IT systems;

Industrialise PoCs once they have proven to bring value-added, in close collaboration with other engineers members of EMPL.A4;

Train custom machine learning (e.g. RAG based) based on structured and unstructured data;

Interact with the local data correspondent, the digital transformation officer, and other IT stakeholders;

Process, cleanse, and verify the integrity of data used for analysis;

Follow the progress of implementation of corporate AI initiatives @EC, and identify opportunities to leverage the use of them within DG EMPL;

Contribute to the analysis of data management vision, strategy and policy and derive the IT requirements.




Requirements:

Excellent knowledge of Python

Excellent knowledge of NLP/ML and common Python libraries (e.g.: PyTorch, SpaCy, NLTK, Scikit-learn, Pandas, Pydantic)

Very good knowledge of package management (e.g.: Poetry, pip, Conda)

Very good knowledge of OOP and functional programming

Very good knowledge of Git

Extensive knowledge of machine learning techniques and algorithms

Extensive knowledge of neural networks and deep learning, with a focus on text processing models

Very good knowledge of prompt engineering techniques

Very good knowledge of models MLOps techniques (model testing/deployment/monitoring/versioning, experiment tracking)

Very good knowledge of Data Management

Good knowledge of statistical analysis, experimental design and large scale data processing

Good knowledge of LLMs and Transformer architecture

Good knowledge of RAG systems

Good knowledge of Azure and/or AWS

Good knowledge of Docker, Kubernetes and IaC (e.g.: knowledge of Bicep and/or Terraform will be considered a plus)

Good knowledge of Linux

Good knowledge of NoSQL databases (e.g.: MongoDB, ElasticSearch)

Good knowledge of vector DB

Good knowledge of query languages (e.g.: SQL, ES, QL)

Good knowledge of agile software development methodologies



Skills:

Ability to participate in multilingual meetings

Ability to understand, speak and write English, optionally French as an additional asset

Excellent interpersonal and communication skills

Ability to work in a team as well as autonomously

Results-oriented mindset, focused on delivering

Good applied statistics skills, such as distributions, statistical testing, regression, etc.

Good scripting and programming skills

Data-driven mindset



Non-Technical Skills:

Capability of integration in an international/multicultural environment, rapid self-starting capability and experience in working in a team

Ability to participate in multilingual meetings

Ability to work in multi-cultural environment, on multiple large projects

Excellent Team Player



Specific Expertise:

Any experience in the field of RAG processing will be considered as an asset

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
As an experienced data scientist, you extract relevant insights from the amount of data available within Colruyt Group. You build machine learning models for our various retail brands. The result of your work: an even better, tailor-made service for our customers.




What will you do?

Within the IT division Data & Analytics, you work in a multidisciplinary team consisting of data scientists, data science engineers, data analysts, BI engineers… Together, you provide analytical solutions for the various Colruyt Group brands, such as Okay, Bike Republic, Bio-Planet or Jims. Within the data science knowledge group, experiences and expertise are shared, allowing you to continue learning.
As a data scientist, you collaborate with complementary colleagues on a wide range of projects, working with new techniques and technologies (e.g., Polars, LightGBM, PyTorch, UV, Kedro, Ruff). In addition, we also work with cutting-edge in-house technology, such as our own forecasting module or causality module. You are also encouraged to proactively explore new methods and techniques—in consultation with the team—to help expand the knowledge and skill set of the group.
The team focuses on our internal customers, the business partners. We translate their needs into analytical solutions, which we develop in multidisciplinary teams. This results in close and direct collaboration with other IT teams and the business.
We are responsible for identifying new analytics opportunities. We proactively search for insights that create added value for our partners. In doing so, we look not only at Colruyt Group, but also at external trends in analytical use cases and technological innovation.
Colruyt Group consists of many different entities, which creates enormous variety and volume in both data and use cases. The projects you contribute to range from customer segmentation, sales forecasting, and analysis of in-store experiments to operational efficiency of machines, predictive maintenance, and optimal use of raw materials.




You work in Halle and can work from home up to two days per week.




Your Profile

Experience – You hold a master’s degree in data science, business engineering, computer science, statistics, mathematics, … and you have at least 3 to 5 years of relevant experience.
Knowledge of statistical modelling – You have excellent knowledge of machine learning and statistical modelling and can implement these in Python. You have a good knowledge of SQL and data visualisation techniques.
Analytical ability – You are capable of translating data into insights and communicating these clearly and concisely.
Language skills – You have excellent command of English and a good knowledge of Dutch.




What We Offer

Stimulating work environment – You join an incredibly fascinating and rich data environment, where you are given room for initiative and gain a broad view of the company.
Continuous learning opportunities – In addition to an extensive range of internal training courses, you also have the opportunity to follow additional training programmes. This allows you to continuously develop your business knowledge, technical skills, and social competencies.
Attractive salary package – You receive a competitive salary with numerous fringe benefits, such as a company car, profit sharing, and meal vouchers.




At Colruyt Group, you can work differently. You, as a person, take centre stage—and you feel it immediately. You can sharpen your skills thanks to our development-oriented approach and grow internally if you wish. Whether you are an analyst, store employee, marketeer or technician, you will surely find a job that fits you perfectly.




Colruyt Group believes in the power of diversity, equality and inclusion. Everyone is welcome to apply, and we offer equal opportunities to all.




------------------------------------------------------------------------------------




Als ervaren data scientist puur je relevante inzichten uit de grote hoeveelheid data waarover Colruyt Group beschikt. Je maakt machine learning modellen voor onze verschillende retailmerken. Het resultaat van jouw werk: een nog betere dienstverlening op maat van onze klanten.




Wat doe je concreet?

In de IT divisie Data & Analytics werk je in een multidisciplinair team bestaande uit data scientists, data science engineers, data analysten, bi engineers… Samen bieden jullie analytische oplossingen aan voor de verschillende Colruyt Group merken, zoals Colruyt Laagste Prijs, Okay, Bike-Republic of Jims. In de data science kennisgroep worden ervaringen en kennis uitgewisseld. Op deze manier blijf je bijleren.
Als data scientist werk je samen met complementaire collega’s aan een brede waaier van projecten waarbij je in aanraking komt met nieuwe technieken en technologieën (bv: Polars, lightgbm, pytorch, uv, kedro, ruff). Daarnaast werken we ook met cutting edge in-house technologie zoals onze eigen forecast module of causaliteitsmodule. We moedigen je ook aan om zelf, in overleg met het team, op zoek te gaan naar nieuwe methoden en technieken en zo de kennis en skills van de groep te vergroten.
Het team focust op onze interne klanten, de business-partners. We vertalen hun noden in analytische oplossingen die we bouwen in multidisciplinaire teams. Dat resulteert in een directe en nauwe samenwerking met andere IT teams en business.
We zijn verantwoordelijk voor de identificatie van nieuwe analytics-opportuniteiten. We gaan proactief op zoek naar nieuwe inzichten die een toegevoegde waarde creëren voor onze partners. Daarbij kijken we uiteraard niet alleen naar Colruyt Group, maar volgen we ook de externe trends op het vlak van use cases rond analytics en technologische innovatie.
Colruyt Group bestaat uit veel verschillende entiteiten. Dat zorgt voor een enorme variatie en hoeveelheid aan data én use cases. De projecten waar je aan meewerkt gaan dan ook van klantensegmentatie over verkoopsprognose en analyse van winkeltesten tot operationele efficiëntie van machines, predictive maintenance en optimaal gebruik van grondstoffen.




Je werkt in Halle en kan tot 2 dagen per week thuiswerken.




Jouw profiel:

Ervaring – Je behaalde een master in data science, handelsingenieur, computerwetenschappen, statistiek, wiskunde,... Je hebt minstens 3 tot 5 jaar relevante ervaring.
Kennis statistische modellering – Je hebt een uitstekende kennis van machine learning en statistische modellering en kan deze implementeren in Python. Je hebt een goede kennis van SQL en datavisualisatie-technieken.
Analytisch vermogen – Je slaagt erin om data te vertalen naar inzichten en je kan die ook helder en duidelijk communiceren.
Talenkennis – Je hebt een uitstekende kennis van het Engels en een goede kennis van het Nederlands.




Wij bieden:

Stimulerende werkomgeving – Je belandt in een ongelooflijk boeiende en rijke data-omgeving, krijgt ruimte voor initiatief en krijgt een brede kijk op het bedrijf.
Permanente leermogelijkheden – Naast een heel ruim aanbod aan interne opleidingen krijg je ook de kans om aanvullende opleidingstrajecten te volgen. Op die manier bouw je continu aan je business-kennis, je technische skills en je sociale vaardigheden.
Aantrekkelijk loonpakket – Je krijgt een competitief loon met tal van extralegale voordelen zoals een bedrijfswagen, winstdeelname en maaltijdcheques.

Bij Colruyt Group kan je anders werken. Jij staat als mens centraal en dat merk je meteen. Zo kan je je vaardigheden scherpstellen dankzij onze ontwikkelingsgerichte aanpak en intern doorgroeien als je dat wilt. Of je nu analyst, winkelmedewerker, marketeer of technieker bent, bij ons vind je vast een job die perfect bij je past.




Colruyt Group gelooft in de kracht van diversiteit, gelijkwaardigheid en inclusie. Iedereen is welkom om te solliciteren bij Colruyt Group en we bieden iedereen gelijke kansen.

Title: Expert in AI/ NLP/ ML
Description:
Job Description

The external service provider will be responsible for the following tasks:

Design, implement, and maintain a scalable, reliable, and secure hybrid cloud ML Ops infrastructure for deploying, testing, managing, and monitoring ML models in various environments.

Develop and maintain software applications in the areas of Natural Language Processing (NLP), Machine Learning (ML), Deep Learning (DL), and/or Artificial Intelligence (AI).

Collaborate closely with data scientists and back-end developers to construct, test, integrate, and deploy ML models.

Analyze performance metrics, troubleshoot issues, and ensure high availability and reliability.

Design CI/CD pipelines, utilize orchestration solutions, and data versioning tools.

Create automated anomaly detection systems, continuously monitor performance, and optimize ML pipelines for scalability, efficiency, and cost-effectiveness.

Architect IT solutions in the NLP/ML/AI domains, considering master- and meta-data management concepts, and coordinate their implementation.

Provide security studies, security assessments, and guidance on information system projects.

Offer support and guidance to other team members on MLOps practices.




Knowledge and Skills Required:

Excellent knowledge of managing an on-prem and/or cloud MLOps infrastructure.

Excellent knowledge of containerization and orchestration platforms (e.g. Kubernetes, Docker, Podman, EKS, PKS).

Good knowledge of MLflow, TensorFlow (TFX) or equivalents.

Good knowledge of Airflow.

Good knowledge of AWS and/or Azure.

Good knowledge of Python.

Good knowledge of Unix and Bash.

Good knowledge agile software development methodologies.

Good knowledge of infrastructure as code (Terraform, CloudFormation).

Good knowledge of messaging services and platforms (e.g. Kafka, Redis, RabbitMQ).

Knowledge of data security measures (knowledge of encryption mechanisms and ML security is considered a plus).

Knowledge of NoSQL databases, such as Elasticsearch, MongoDB, Cassandra, HBase, etc.

Knowledge of query languages, such as SQL, Hive, Pig, etc. and with information extraction.

Experience with data analytics over big datasets, non-structured databases as well as data lakes.

Experience with monitoring and logging tools (e.g. ELK stack, Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry, Cloudwatch).

Experience with model testing and model validation in production environments.

Ability to write clear and structured technical documentation.

Excellent knowledge of on-prem or cloud solutions for data science applications.

Ability to give business and technical presentations.

Ability to apply high-quality standards.

Ability to cope with fast-changing technologies.

Very good communication skills with technical and non-technical audiences.

Analysis and problem-solving skills.

Capability to write clear and structured technical documents.

Ability to participate in technical meetings and good communication skills.




Optional Certifications:

AWS Certified Machine Learning.

Microsoft Azure AI Engineer Associate.

  • Good knowledge of Unix and Bash.

  • Good knowledge agile software development methodologies.

  • Good knowledge of infrastructure as code (Terraform, CloudFormation).

  • Good knowledge of messaging services and platforms (e.g. Kafka, Redis, RabbitMQ).

  • Knowledge of data security measures (knowledge of encryption mechanisms and ML security is considered a plus).

  • Knowledge of NoSQL databases, such as Elasticsearch, MongoDB, Cassandra, HBase, etc.

  • Knowledge of query languages, such as SQL, Hive, Pig, etc. and with information extraction.

  • Experience with data analytics over big datasets, non-structured databases as well as data lakes.

  • Experience with monitoring and logging tools (e.g. ELK stack, Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry, Cloudwatch).

  • Experience with model testing and model validation in production environments.

  • Ability to write clear and structured technical documentation.

  • Excellent knowledge of on-prem or cloud solutions for data science applications.

  • Ability to give business and technical presentations.

  • Ability to apply high-quality standards.

  • Ability to cope with fast-changing technologies.

  • Very good communication skills with technical and non-technical audiences.

  • Analysis and problem-solving skills.

  • Capability to write clear and structured technical documents.

  • Ability to participate in technical meetings and good communication skills.


Optional Certifications:

  • AWS Certified Machine Learning.

  • Microsoft Azure AI Engineer Associate.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Wat jij mag verwachten

In deze functie ontwikkel en industrialiseer je AI‑oplossingen die een directe impact hebben

op kernprocessen binnen een sterk gereguleerde omgeving, in nauwe samenwerking met

AI Engineers, Product Owners, architecten en businessstakeholders.




Agile Way of Working

Je maakt deel uit van een multidisciplinair AI‑team en werkt volgens een agile manier van werken.
Je vertaalt businessvragen naar concrete AI‑usecases met heldere hypotheses, succescriteria en acceptatievoorwaarden.
Je stemt actief af met Product Owner, DPM, architectuur en businessstakeholders.
Je documenteert aannames, ontwerpkeuzes en resultaten zodat oplossingen
uitlegbaar, herbruikbaar en auditproof blijven.




AI Delivery

DataScience & Machine Learning (solution development)

Ontwikkelen van ML-modellen voor concrete businessproblemen zoals: fraudedetectie, risico-inschatting en classificatie en voorspellingen
Uitvoeren van data-exploratie, feature engineering en modelselectie.
Valideren van modellen op basis van performance, stabiliteit, bias en explainability.
End-to-endverantwoordelijkheid voor AI-oplossingen: productie implementatie, monitoring en evaluatie, iteratieve verbetering en (waar nodig) finetuning gedurende de volledige lifecycle.
Definiëren van drift, kwaliteits- en performance-indicatoren.




Agentic & Applied AI Solutions

Je ontwerpt en ontwikkelt agentic en applied AI-oplossingen vooroperationele processen, zoals:

document intelligence
(classificatie, extractie, samenvatten)
AI-ondersteuning voor interne workflows
Je werkt met -, prompts en tool use om betrouwbare oplossingen te bouwen.
Je test en evalueert agentgedrag via scenario’s, testsets en offline evaluaties.
Je werkt nauw samen met AI Engineers/Architects bij de integratie van agentic oplossingen in productieflows.
Je denkt mee over human-in-the-loop en guardrails passendbij risico en regelgeving.




AI Strategy

Je denkt mee over usecaseselectie en prioritering op basis van impact, haalbaarheid en risico.
Je draagt bij aan het verder uitwerken van AI-richtlijnen en best practices (modelgebruik, evaluatie, documentatie).
Je houdt rekening met AI governance en EU AI-principes (transparantie, uitlegbaarheid, fairness).
Je deelt kennis en inzichten met collega’s en stimuleert AI literacy binnen de organisatie.
Je volgt ontwikkelingen in ML en agentic AI en vertaalt die naar praktische toepassingen.




Wat wij verwachten

Je beschikt over een masterdiploma of hebt een gelijkwaardig denkniveau in Data Science, AI, Computer Science, Engineering of een verwante richting.
Je kunt ruime ervaring als Data Scientist, Applied AI Specialist of in een vergelijkbare rol aantonen.
Je bent bedreven in Python en hebt ervaring met gangbare datascience-libraries zoals pandas, scikit-learn, PyTorch of TensorFlow.
Je hebt ervaring met end-to-endmodelontwikkeling, van data-analyse tot validatie.
Je hebt affiniteit met agentic AI, LLMs en RAG-oplossingen.
Je kent de concepten van MLOps en werkt graag samen om modellen naar productie te brengen.
Je hebt oog voor data privacy, ethiek en AI governance.
Je spreekt vloeiend Nederlands en Engels.
Je bent analytisch sterk, nieuwsgierig, communicatief vaardig en gefocust op het creëren van businessimpact.




Waarom deze rol uniek is?

Je werktaan concrete AI-oplossingen met zichtbare impact in kernprocessen (fraude, risico, operations, documentverwerking).
Je combineert klassieke data science met moderne agentic AI-toepassingen.
Je helpt mee om AI structureel te verankeren binnen de organisatie.




Klaar om AI-oplossingen te bouwen die dagelijks gebruikt worden?

Solliciteer en maak impact met data en AI.

Title: Software Engineer
Description:
Senior Java Engineer

Brussels, Belgium

Long term contract




Overview

Our client is seeking a Senior Java Engineer with strong expertise in modern Java, cloud‑native development, and high‑throughput system optimisation. You will join a backend engineering team working on large‑scale, performance‑critical applications deployed in public cloud environments. The focus of the role is to build, tune, and optimise Java services to ensure reliability, scalability, and excellent runtime performance.

This position is ideal for someone who enjoys deep technical problem‑solving, distributed systems, and hands‑on performance engineering.




Key Responsibilities

Design, develop, and maintain Java backend applications using modern frameworks and best practices.
Build and optimise services using Java (JDK 21+), Java SE, Spring Boot, JMS, REST, and SQL.
Implement high‑quality CI/CD pipelines with Jenkins, including unit, integration, and automated test orchestration.
Work within containerised environments using Docker and Kubernetes.
Develop and tune data‑intensive services interacting with Oracle and PostgreSQL databases.
Design and maintain distributed microservices and event‑driven systems using Kafka and REST APIs.
Operate and troubleshoot applications running on public cloud platforms (Azure preferred; AWS also relevant).
Use infrastructure‑as‑code tooling such as Terraform to support cloud deployments.
Apply strong monitoring, observability, and networking principles to ensure system stability.
Perform performance profiling and tuning of high‑throughput, low‑latency applications.
Collaborate closely with engineering teams to solve complex technical challenges.
Contribute to technical discussions, architecture decisions, and best‑practice improvements.




Required Skills & Experience

Technical Skills

Strong experience in Java application development.
Excellent knowledge of:
Java JDK 21+, Java SE
Spring Boot
JMS, REST, SQL
Practical experience with Jenkins, CI pipelines, unit and integration testing, and test orchestration.
Advanced knowledge of Docker and Kubernetes environments.
Very good understanding of Oracle and PostgreSQL databases.
Comfortable working in Linux environments.
Knowledge of Kafka, RESTful APIs, and microservices architecture.
Familiarity with public cloud platforms (Azure preferred) including Terraform, Kubernetes, networking, and monitoring tools.
Hands‑on experience tuning high‑throughput, large‑scale systems deployed on cloud platforms (Azure or AWS).

Title: Software Engineer (In dienst of freelance)
Description:
Taken en verantwoordelijkheden

Functietitel: Software Engineer

Werkkader

Je komt terecht in een gespecialiseerde organisatie binnen de publieke sector, waar softwareoplossingen worden ontwikkeld ter ondersteuning van operationele noden.

Functiebeschrijving

Je vervoegt een bestaand team van vier personen dat werkt aan de ontwikkeling van een Python-applicatie in Django. Je staat in voor de ontwikkeling van nieuwe features en het onderhoud van bestaande functionaliteiten. Het project wordt uitgewerkt volgens Agile-methoden en draait volledig on-premise.


Ontwikkelen van nieuwe functionaliteiten in een bestaande Django-applicatie
Onderhouden en verbeteren van bestaande softwarecomponenten
Vertalen van gebruikers- en klantnoden naar technische vereisten
Samenwerken in een Agile team van vier personen
Bijdragen aan betrouwbare en data-gedreven oplossingen met aandacht voor gegevenskwaliteit en integriteit



Er wordt een aanwezigheid van 2 dagen per week op kantoor verwacht, op dinsdag en donderdag, in Brussel. Je moet een veiligheidsaccreditatie kunnen verkrijgen.

Profiel

We zoeken een gedreven en detailgerichte software engineer met een passie voor het modelleren, ontwerpen en ontwikkelen van innovatieve softwarecomponenten en applicaties. Je hebt ervaring met het ontwikkelen van data-applicaties in Django en aantoonbare ervaring in het vertalen van noden naar technische vereisten.

Technische vaardigheden


Front-end: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React
Back-end: Python, Django
Data: Python, SQL, pandas
API development: REST, SOAP, GraphQL
DevOps: Docker, Docker Compose, CI/CD
Versioning: Git, GitHub, GitLab
Cloud platforms: Azure is een plus
Extra pluspunt: ervaring met Nginx of Apache webservers



Opleiding en ervaring


Minimum een bachelor in Software Development, IT of gelijkwaardige aantoonbare ervaring
Aantoonbare ervaring in applicatieontwikkeling met Python
Ervaring met Django en data-applicaties



Competenties


Sterke focus op gegevenskwaliteit, nauwkeurigheid en betrouwbaarheid
Uitstekende analytische en probleemoplossende vaardigheden
Sterke communicatievaardigheden
Teamgericht en collaboratief
Innovatief denkvermogen
Oog voor detail
Proactieve werkhouding



Talen


Engels
Beheerst één van de landstalen (Nederlands of Frans), bij voorkeur beide
Indien niet, is minimaal een passief begrip van de andere landstaal vereist



Aanbod

Je zal deel uitmaken van een groeiende KMO met ruimte voor initiatief en persoonlijke ontwikkeling. Wij zorgen ervoor dat je in een uitdagende maar aangename werkomgeving terecht komt met leuke collega's. Samen met jou stippelen we een carriereplan uit, met aandacht en budget voor bijkomende opleidingingen / certificaties. Je kan rekenen op een motiverend salaris, aangevuld met extralegale voordelen, inclusief bedrijfswagen (of alternatieve vergoeding).

(Freelance is ook mogelijk)

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
🚀 Data Scientist (Computer Vision & NLP) | Relocate to Porto, Portugal | 1–4 Years Experience




Hey future innovator 👋

We’re on the hunt for a Data Scientist who’s obsessed with solving real-world problems using Computer Vision, NLP, and Deep Learning. If you’ve got 1–4 years of experience, right to work in EU, and a passion for building cool stuff with Python, we want to hear from you.




**You must be willing to relocate to Porto, Portugal - full relocation support**




Why work in iGaming with US?

Because you'll be building cutting-edge tech that powers entertainment for millions—where innovation meets impact, and your work directly shapes the future of digital gaming experiences.




What you’ll be doing:

Building smart ML models using image, text, audio & tabular data
Training pipelines in Azure and deploying end-to-end apps in Python
Creating tools for model inference, logging, and performance tracking
Collaborating with data and engineering teams to solve complex challenges
Staying ahead of the curve with the latest in LLMs, RAG, and CV/NLP trends




What you bring:

Solid Python skills + experience with PyTorch or TensorFlow
Hands-on with Docker, CI/CD, and testing frameworks like Pytest
Experience with model tracking, versioning, and performance metrics
Bonus points if you’ve worked with Azure ML, Huggingface, or LangChain




What’s in it for you:

💸 Competitive salary
📚 Training on industry best practices
🌍 Remote-first culture with a global team
🚀 Growth opportunities in a fast-moving tech company




If you’re ready to level up your career and build cutting-edge solutions with a team that values innovation, apply now! 🌟




Remote (EU only) | Must have right to work

Title: Data Scientist H/F
Description:
MCA BELGIUM, filiale du groupe français MCA, est une société de conseil en ingénierie et hautes technologies. Avec plus de 15 ans d’expérience en Belgique, nous accompagnons nos clients dans la réalisation de projets industriels complexes dans divers secteurs d’activité (énergie, nucléaire, pharmaceutique, aéronautique, ferroviaire).

Nous rejoindre, c’est intégrer une entreprise dynamique qui place l’accompagnement de ses collaborateurs au cœur de ses priorités. Vous bénéficierez d’un suivi personnalisé et de nombreuses formations pour développer vos compétences.

Votre Rôle

Rattaché(e) au pôle Data & Engineering, vous intervenez sur l’exploitation avancée de données industrielles issues d’équipements d’énergies renouvelables (solaire & éolien), avec pour objectif d’optimiser la performance des installations et d’anticiper les défaillances :


Analyse de données provenant de machines industrielles (production, capteurs, historiques de fonctionnement)
Conception et développement de modèles statistiques et algorithmiques (machine learning, détection d’anomalies)
Identification des pertes de performance, anomalies et risques de panne
Participation à la mise en place de solutions de maintenance prédictive
Contribution à l’optimisation du potentiel des équipements
Restitution des résultats via rapports techniques et documentation associée
Collaboration avec les équipes multidisciplinaires (ingénierie, experts terrain, métiers)
Veille sur les évolutions technologiques et méthodologiques en data science appliquée à l’industrie


Votre Profil


Diplôme d’ingénieur ou formation équivalente, idéalement avec une spécialisation en data science, mathématiques appliquées ou domaine proche
Première expérience professionnelle en data (stage long, VIE ou premier poste accepté), avec exposition concrète à des projets data
Maîtrise de Python
Bonne compréhension des algorithmes, statistiques et approches machine learning
Esprit analytique, rigueur et sens du travail en équipe
Maîtrise du français & anglais professionnel


Vos Perspectives D’évolution

Nous souhaitons vous accompagner dans votre montée en compétences. Notre objectif est de vous donner toutes les clés pour réussir et accéder aux différentes opportunités d’évolution. Vous pourrez évoluer vers des fonctions techniques, de gestion de projet ou de management. Ce poste est ouvert aux personnes en situation de handicap.

  • Conception et développement de modèles statistiques et algorithmiques (machine learning, détection d’anomalies)
  • Identification des pertes de performance, anomalies et risques de panne
  • Participation à la mise en place de solutions de maintenance prédictive
  • Contribution à l’optimisation du potentiel des équipements
  • Restitution des résultats via rapports techniques et documentation associée
  • Collaboration avec les équipes multidisciplinaires (ingénierie, experts terrain, métiers)
  • Veille sur les évolutions technologiques et méthodologiques en data science appliquée à l’industrie

Votre Profil

  • Diplôme d’ingénieur ou formation équivalente, idéalement avec une spécialisation en data science, mathématiques appliquées ou domaine proche
  • Première expérience professionnelle en data (stage long, VIE ou premier poste accepté), avec exposition concrète à des projets data
  • Maîtrise de Python
  • Bonne compréhension des algorithmes, statistiques et approches machine learning
  • Esprit analytique, rigueur et sens du travail en équipe
  • Maîtrise du français & anglais professionnel

Vos Perspectives D’évolution

Nous souhaitons vous accompagner dans votre montée en compétences. Notre objectif est de vous donner toutes les clés pour réussir et accéder aux différentes opportunités d’évolution. Vous pourrez évoluer vers des fonctions techniques, de gestion de projet ou de management. Ce poste est ouvert aux personnes en situation de handicap.

Title: Forecasting Data Scientist
Description:
Are you a Data Scientist with strong forecasting experience who’s comfortable taking models from concept through to production?

This is an opportunity to work on real-world forecasting use cases within a large Energy organisation, where your work will directly support pricing, demand planning, and operational decisions.

You’ll be part of a cross-functional product team, working closely with Product Managers, Data Engineers, and Architects to build and deploy scalable data science solutions that are actually used - not just explored.




Your Role as a Data Scientist (Forecasting):

You will focus on building and delivering forecasting models that solve concrete business problems. This role spans the full lifecycle - from understanding requirements through to deployment and ongoing optimisation.

You’ll work closely with both technical and business stakeholders to ensure models are accurate, reliable, and aligned with how decisions are made.




What You’ll Do:

Develop and implement forecasting models using classical/statistical approaches (time series, regression-based methods)
Translate business problems (pricing, demand, parts forecasting) into practical data science solutions
Collaborate closely with Product Managers to align model outputs with business needs
Work alongside Data Engineers to ensure robust data pipelines and production-ready deployment
Contribute to MLOps practices including deployment, monitoring, and model lifecycle management
Engage with stakeholders to understand context and refine requirements
Partner with architects to align solutions with system architecture and scalability goals
Continuously improve model performance based on real-world usage




What You’ll Bring:

Strong experience in forecasting and time series modelling (classical/statistical approaches)
Proficiency in Python for data science and model development
Hands-on experience with AWS SageMaker for model development and deployment
Experience with Amazon Bedrock or similar platforms
Understanding of MLOps practices (CI/CD, model versioning, monitoring, deployment pipelines)
Experience working with production data systems and collaborating with Data Engineers
Ability to translate business problems into structured, data-driven solutions




Why Join the Remobi community:

Fully remote, b2b/freelance contracts
Opportunity to work on large-scale, high-impact transformation programmes
Access to a network of experienced data, AI, and engineering professionals across Europe




👉 Apply today:

If you’re comfortable owning forecasting models end-to-end and getting them into production, this is a strong fit.

Title: Applied AI Data Scientist (CEO Office)
Description:
TheSoul Group is a leading creator business platform positioned at the heart of the creator economy. Our original videos, animations, and editorial stories inspire audiences in 21 languages, across 60 platforms and 20 websites.

Together with our network of 15,000 talented creators, we reach over 5 billion followers and generate more than 100 billion monthly views on social media.

As a global powerhouse in digital media, we drive viral success for creators and brands worldwide through a wide range of services, leveraging our expertise in content distribution, localization, and social media management. We're the creators behind global phenomena such as 5-Minute Crafts and BrightSide, and we continue to expand our influence in the creator economy through strategic acquisitions like Mediacube and Underscore Talent.

We're a dynamic, remote-first global team, fostering award-winning creativity, a no-red-tape approach, and great vibes!

We are seeking an exceptional Applied AI Data Scientist to join our team at the forefront of content intelligence. You will explore the world's largest dataset on content performance—over 100 billion views per month across 50+ platforms—to uncover hidden insights and drive measurable impact. Your work will power AI agents, automate analytics, and directly shape high-level business strategy, reporting to the CEO. This is a rare opportunity to combine advanced AI research with real-world product and business influence.

Responsibilities


Analyze and interpret metrics like CTR, retention, engagement, and user behavior at an unprecedented scale (100B+ views/month).
Build robust and reproducible analytical workflows to uncover actionable insights.
Validate and optimize measurement methods to ensure accuracy across platforms.
Identify non-obvious correlations, causal relationships, and latent patterns in large, complex datasets.
Translate statistical findings into strategic recommendations for content, product, and growth teams.
Challenge assumptions and propose innovative analytical frameworks.
Design, build, and maintain AI agents that automate routine and complex analytical processes.
Experiment with LLMs, reinforcement learning, and other generative AI techniques to accelerate insights.
Deploy AI solutions in production to improve decision-making speed and accuracy.
Develop end-to-end machine learning pipelines for large-scale data processing.
Ensure code quality, reproducibility, and scalability for production environments.
Continuously monitor, test, and optimize ML/DL models for performance and robustness.
Present findings and strategic recommendations directly to the CEO.
Influence high-level business and content strategy with data-driven insights.
Communicate complex technical results in a clear, actionable manner for non-technical stakeholders.


Requirements


Professional experience as a Data Scientist, Machine Learning Engineer, or Applied AI researcher, with a track record of deploying models or building analytical systems in production
Russian language fluency and strong English language proficiency for reporting, collaboration, and presenting insights
Proven top-tier competitive coding credentials: Kaggle Master+, ICPC medalist, IOI medalist, or Codeforces IM+
Strong programming skills with production-ready Python and SQL experience
Deep understanding of machine learning and deep learning, beyond standard boosting models
Intellectual curiosity and ownership mentality—you take full responsibility for problem-solving end-to-end
Experience turning complex datasets into actionable insights for business or product strategy


Nice-to-have


Experience working with large-scale datasets (PB-scale) and distributed systems
Hands-on experience building or deploying LLMs, AI agents, or advanced automation systems
Knowledge of content, media, or social platform analytics


Benefits


Flexible Engagement Structure: This opportunity is offered under an independent service-provider agreement, giving you the freedom to shape how you deliver your expertise. You define your own working methods, organise your schedule independently, and contribute your skills to clearly scoped projects. Compensation is agreed individually and reflects your experience, capabilities, and the value you bring to each engagement
Location Freedom: You can deliver services from any suitable location that supports high-quality outcomes, whether that's your home office, a co-working space, or wherever you feel most productive and inspired. What matters most is the impact you create and the reliability of your delivery
Global Network and Inspiring Projects: You'll collaborate with a diverse international network of professionals across 85+ countries, contributing to projects that reach vast audiences and help shape the future of digital content. This is an opportunity to work alongside talented specialists, exchange ideas, and be part of meaningful initiatives with real global reach
Smooth Collaboration and Project Support: To enable efficient service delivery, we provide modern collaboration platforms, clear project coordination, and streamlined processes. This helps you focus your energy where it matters most, producing high-impact work and bringing creative solutions to life
Make a Global Impact: Your expertise will contribute to initiatives that influence audiences worldwide. Join projects that push creative boundaries and help build the next generation of digital experiences.


We appreciate your interest in our job opportunities and our company. Our team carefully evaluates each application to identify the most suitable candidates for the role. Due to the high volume of applications received, we may not be able to respond to every applicant. However, if your qualifications align with our requirements, we will contact you to discuss the next steps in the selection process.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
YOUR JOB IN A NUTSHELL

The Tribe Artificial Intelligence aims to deliver an efficient and seamless banking experience for our customers and empower our employees through AI. Our expert team of approximately 100 experts includes Data Scientists, AI Engineers, Business Analysts, Scrum Masters, Product Owners and Managers. We are seeking a Data Scientist with a minimum of 3 years of experience in developing AI applications, including both generative and traditional AI.




AND IN DETAIL

You develop AI applications to address various business challenges, including virtual assistants to enhance client experiences, advanced AI tools to support employee efficiency and creativity, and automating and optimizing internal processes.
You use various analytical methods, including large language models (prompt engineering, RAG, fine-tuning, …), Speech AI, agentic AI, image generation and interpretation, and classical statistical/machine learning techniques. While we don't expect you to be an expert in all areas, you should possess very strong expertise in core technologies and the ability to learn new methods as required.
You ensure scientific rigor in delivered solutions by applying robust methodologies, evaluating model generalization performance, adopting data-centric approaches through error analysis and data curation or augmentation, selecting relevant frameworks, and adhering to responsible AI guideline.
You present the results of your work directly to stakeholders and managers.
You contribute to deploying AI applications in production using a comprehensive stack of tools.
You access one of the richest volumes of varied data in the market and apply your skills to it.
You evolve in an environment where innovation and lean processes are praised, straight-forward communication is encouraged, and peers understand the meaning of team up.
You work with a team of colleagues in an Agile organization who are ready to collaborate and to share their experience.




YOUR TALENTS AND COMPETENCES

You hold at least a Master’s degree in a quantitative field like statistics, computer science or engineering, with a focus on natural language processing. A PhD is advantageous. Candidates from other academic backgrounds with strong analytics knowledge are also welcome.
You have a minimum of 3 years’ experience in data science, preferably in the financial sector, though less experience may be accepted if your academic achievements are substantial.
You are fluent in English and have a good command of French and/or Dutch.
You are proficient in Python for model building and understand ML engineering fundamentals (e.g., versioning systems) to produce code ready for CI/CD pipelines.




WHAT WE HAVE TO OFFER

OUR IMPACT

We strongly believe in the positive impact that the financial sector can have on the world. BNP Paribas Fortis offers sustainable and responsible investment products and solutions and encourages social entrepreneurship. Together with our customers, we are working towards a better future every day.




YOUR CAREER PERSPECTIVES

We offer a wide range of training courses, workshops and webinars to choose from depending on your availability, interests and goals.




OUR OFFER

In addition to your remuneration and benefits (meal vouchers, insurance, holidays), we offer flexible salary options, allowing you to convert part of your salary into personalised benefits (phone use, IT equipment, transport, etc.).




OUR ENGAGEMENT

At BNP Paribas Fortis, we want to attract and retain all talent, whatever their gender, age, background or sexual orientation, and irrespective of whether they are living with a disability, as every person has their own experiences and their own identity. All of our full-time vacancies are also open to candidates wishing to work on an 80% or 90% full-time equivalent basis.




If the function is categorized as a banking services provider function (cf. Law of 22 April 2019 establishing an oath and a disciplinary regime for the banking sector), you will also need to provide us with a 'certificate of absence of professional ban' from the FSMA confirming that you are not professionally disqualified.




TEMPTED BY THE CHALLENGE?

A first selection is made based on your CV and motivation letter. Are we convinced that you are the right candidate? Then we will contact you. Good luck!




Is this position not for you, but do you know someone who is cut out for this job? Feel free to share it with friends and family!

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
We’re looking for a Data Scientist to join our tech team and help build the future of neuro-contextual advertising at global scale.

Who We Are

At Seedtag, our mission is to transform advertising by proving that effectiveness and user privacy can truly coexist.

As the leading Neuro-Contextual Advertising Company, we combine Artificial Intelligence, Natural Language Processing, Computer Vision, and neuroscience to understand not only what content is about, but how it makes people feel and what they intend to do next.

Our proprietary AI, Liz, enables brands to connect with audiences across the open web and Connected TV without cookies or user tracking. Founded in 2014 by two ex-Googlers, Seedtag has grown to 700+ Seedtaggers in 17 countries, backed by €250M in funding, and operates today as a global ad-tech leader.

If you enjoy solving complex engineering challenges and building AI-driven systems at scale, you’ll feel right at home here.

Your Challenge

As Data Scientist, You Will


As a member of our data science team, you will build production-grade AI agent systems that interact with data, execute multi-step workflows, and make autonomous decisions impacting business outcomes.
You will design and implement agent architectures using modern frameworks, working on multi-agent orchestration patterns, agent delegation, and ensuring reliable execution under production constraints.
You will solve context management challenges across conversation history, tool outputs, and file attachments, implementing semantic search and retrieval systems to ensure agents have the right information to answer questions accurately.
You will integrate agents with data infrastructure, connecting them to databases and APIs in real-time, and implement tool integration systems that enable scalable development across teams using protocols like MCP.
You will build evaluation frameworks to measure agent performance in production, taking solutions from concept to deployment, and collaborating closely with cross-functional teams to align your work with business goals.


Our Core Values

Outcome over Output

We measure success by impact and value, not by volume of features or lines of code.

Failure Is Allowed, Learning Is a Must

Experimentation is key to innovation. We test early, iterate often, and learn fast.

We Are All Scouts

We take ownership and leave things better than we found them.

We Are Data-Driven

Data informs our decisions and helps us continuously improve our systems and results.

Tech Stack

We operate at large scale, supporting up to 120k requests per second, with ML models responding in under 10 milliseconds and processing 20 TB of data daily.

Our Stack Includes


Languages: Python, Go, TypeScript, Scala
Cloud & Infrastructure: Kubernetes, GCP, AWS
Streaming: Kafka, Kinesis
Data: Datalakes, GCS, Redis, Druid, MongoDB
Architecture: 100+ microservices, DevOps-driven, continuous deployment


What You’ll Need to Succeed


You bring 3-5 years of experience building and deploying production ML systems, with hands-on experience developing LLM-based applications or autonomous agents using frameworks like Pydantic AI, OpenAI Agents SDK, LangGraph, or similar, and the ability to adapt as the ecosystem evolves.
You possess strong software engineering skills with proficiency in Python and a track record of deploying services to production, including experience with async programming, API design, and production infrastructure like Docker and Kubernetes.
You have a strong academic background in fields such as Computer Science, Engineering, Statistics, Mathematics, or similar, with a focus on quantitative analysis and problem-solving, plus understanding of retrieval systems, embeddings, or context management patterns.
You have hands-on experience with end-to-end project workflows from design to deployment, understand multi-step agent workflows and tool calling patterns, and can handle the non-deterministic nature of LLM-based systems.
You are proactive, entrepreneurial, and adaptable, comfortable working in a fast-moving environment where best practices are still emerging, and committed to building reliable AI agent systems that drive measurable business outcomes.


Why Join Seedtag?


A key moment of growth with real ownership and global impact.
Flexible work model with 100% remote or hybrid options. (Remote contracts available in Spain, Italy, UK, Belgium, Netherlands, France, and Germany.)
Continuous learning through a learning platform and optional language classes.
A supportive, trust-based culture that values well-being.
Team activities, offsites, and opportunities to connect beyond work.


Additional Perks


Home office setup budget up to €1,000
Paid trips to our HQ in Madrid
MacBook Pro M3


Ready to Join the Seedtag Adventure?

At Seedtag, we create an environment where everyone can thrive. If you need accommodations during the hiring process, let us know and we’ll ensure a positive experience.

Send us your CV and let’s build the future of neuro-contextual advertising together.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer (BE)
Description:
Job Description

At CluePoints, we’re redefining how clinical trials are run. As the premier provider of Risk-Based Quality Management (RBQM) and Data Quality Oversight software, we harness advanced statistics, artificial intelligence, and machine learning to ensure the quality, accuracy, and integrity of clinical trial data, helping life sciences organizations bring safer, more effective treatments to patients faster.

We’re proud to be an ambitious, fast-growing technology scale-up with a dynamic and diverse international team representing more than 40 nationalities. Collaboration, flexibility, and continuous learning are part of our DNA.

At CluePoints, you’ll find a culture where you can grow, make an impact, and have fun along the way. Guided by our values of Care, Passion, and Smart Disruption, we’re united by a shared mission: to create smarter ways to run efficient clinical trials and deliver AI-powered insights that improve human outcomes worldwide.

We’re looking for a Machine Learning Engineer to join our team and contribute to our mission of transforming clinical research through data-driven insights.

Job Requirements

Main Qualifications


Master or PhD in Computer sciences or quantitative fields (maths, physics, ...)
Professional experience in machine learning / data science
Efficient in Python
Good knowledge in at least one deep learning platform like TensorFlow or PyTorch
Experience with code versioning tools such as Git


Preferred Qualifications:


Experience with deep learning and natural language Processing
Experience in supporting deployments of machine learning models
Experience with medical data
Experience in GPU computing


Job Responsibilities

As a member of our Research team:


You develop new machine learning models to enhance the CluePoints web platform and/or to develop new products.
You identify promising ML techniques from scientific literature and assess their viability
You leverage relevant open-source resources and keep yourself up-to-date to new developments in this area
You produce robust and clean python code
You follow ML best practices to keep track of experiments and models
You identify new fields of applications for machine learning and deep learning within CluePoints and contribute to increased adoption of machine learning within the healthcare industry
You stay on the edge of relevant research and help CluePoints to continue increasing its scientific visibility by participating at scientific conferences and publishing in scientific journals


Job Benefits

🇧🇪 What We Offer – Belgium


Health Insurance through Alan (100% hospitalisation cover, 80% ambulatory and dental)
Mobility Budget for eco-transport, housing, or car allowance (flexible 3-pillar system)
Group Insurance Plan with 6–12% employer pension contribution based on seniority
Meal Vouchers (€8/day) and Eco Vouchers for sustainable purchases
A hub-based hybrid model that blends flexibility with purpose — connecting teams through collaboration, learning, and a vibrant social culture.


Equal Opportunities & GDPR Notice

CluePoints is an equal opportunities employer. We value and respect diversity in our workforce and do not tolerate discrimination based on gender, age, disability, ethnic origin, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected ground under Belgian law.

Personal data collected as part of your application will be processed in compliance with the EU GDPR and Belgian data protection legislation.

You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data at any time by contacting privacy@cluepoints.com.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
🤖 The Job

At Dataroots, we couple our excellent consulting services with our technical expertise to build advanced machine learning models to improve business processes across a variety of industries, with a strong focus on DataOps and MLOps. As a Machine Learning Engineer, you'll be an integral part of our in-house team of AI specialists, designing and implementing models that deliver robust, data-driven solutions for our clients.




Passionate about data, you enjoy diving deep to uncover actionable insights and develop machine learning models that enhance client operations. You’ll experiment with different approaches, while collaborating with Data Engineers, Cloud Architects, and Data Strategists to drive the delivery of tailored, high-quality, end-to-end solutions.




You’ll also have the opportunity to mentor colleagues, share knowledge, and stay on top of industry developments through a range of initiatives we support internally.




🎉 What you'll find at Dataroots

Since 2016, Dataroots has been committing its excellent consulting services to help clients excel in their data-driven operations. Our approach focuses on team well-being, fostering diversity, and promoting knowledge sharing. By focusing on these values, we've achieved healthy and sustainable growth over the years. Through our consulting services, we actively encourage professional growth by exposing our employees to different industries, tools, technologies,... broadening their scope in the process.

As part of the Talan Group, we are able to take this approach to the next level, adding the element of international mobility to our employees’ career journey.

Join a team where your work will have a direct impact on both our clients and our journey. Learn more about our DNA at [https://dataroots.io/our-dna].




💼 What we’re looking for

You bring the following qualities & experiences to the role:

A deep passion for Machine Learning and a Master’s or PhD in a quantitative discipline.
Strong foundation in software design principles.
A minimum of 2 years of experience in machine learning and big data.
Expertise in machine learning and deep learning, with both a theoretical and practical grasp.
Proficiency in Python & SQL is a must; knowledge of R is an advantage.
Experience with deploying machine learning models and familiarity with MLOps practices.
At least a first experience with AWS, Azure or GCP and a solid understanding of cloud engineering and its use in Machine Learning.
Experience with ML versioning tooling.
Enthusiasm for working with large datasets and a pragmatic approach to problem-solving.
Strong communication skills and the ability to simplify complex technical concepts for clients.
Fluency in English and Dutch.




Don't worry if you don’t meet every requirement from the start. Most of all we are looking for colleagues with the same passion for personal and team development as the rest of us.




💰 The Offer

An attractive salary with extralegal benefits, including:

+ Mobility budget or a company car with fuel/charging card

+ Hospitalisation and group insurance

+ High-end laptop

+ Smartphone with subscription

+ Substantial amount of holidays

+ Meal vouchers

+ ...

Diverse and welcoming work environment, where you’ll collaborate & unwind with colleagues from different cultures and disciplines. The organization of both fun & professional events and initiatives is actively encouraged and supported.
A training budget for individual and team learning opportunities.
Tons of team-building events and sports initiatives to stay connected and unwind.




📍 Where you’ll work

Our main offices are in Leuven and Ghent, with co-working spaces in Charleroi and Antwerp. These spaces are perfect for both focusing and unwinding with colleagues, as well as brainstorming sessions and team events. While much of your work can be performed remotely, you will also work on-site with clients.




🎯 Ready to join Dataroots?

Excited to shape the future of AI as a Machine Learning Engineer with us? We’re looking forward to hearing from you! Apply now and start a new chapter with Dataroots! 🎉
