Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
About the Role

ShowHeroes is a global leader in CTV and digital video advertising solutions. Through premium publisher partnerships, advanced contextual targeting technology, and innovative creative formats, the company helps brands and publishers build meaningful audience connections across all screens.

Powered by its SemanticHero AI platform, ShowHeroes delivers highly relevant, privacy-conscious advertising, and video solutions at scale. Founded in 2016 and headquartered in Berlin, the company operates internationally across Europe, Latin America, and Asia.




We are seeking a skilled and autonomous ML Engineer for a one-year contract with the possibility of extension. Alternatively, the role may be offered on a freelance basis, depending on location and legal compliance. You will work on one of the company's flagship products, a system our team has taken from early-stage research to a robust, highly optimised production engine, and most recently the first on the platform to ship an agent-based feature, contributing across the full ML lifecycle, from research to production.

This is a hands-on role in a small, multicultural team distributed across Italy, Germany, and Sweden. You will collaborate closely with engineers, researchers, and the product manager, and be expected to both build robust ML systems and communicate your work clearly to non-technical stakeholders.




What You'll Take Charge Of

Design, develop, and deploy ML models across NLP, Computer Vision, and classical ML tasks
Write clean, tested, production-grade Python code
Contribute to backend services and data pipelines supporting ML features
Translate vague business problems into concrete technical approaches and production-ready systems
Document your work and research findings in a way that is accessible to a mixed technical and non-technical audience
Participate actively in team discussions, technical reviews, and brainstorming sessions — all conducted remotely




What We're Looking For

ML Core

You bring 2–3 years of hands-on experience in machine learning.
Strong expertise in Natural Language Processing (Transformers, NER, translations), Computer Vision (object detection, classification), and classical algorithms (classification, clustering, regression)
Advanced Python proficiency with a focus on clean, maintainable, and efficient code, including testing

Engineering & DevOps

Solid backend development experience and working knowledge of database management and architecture
Hands-on experience with Docker for building consistent and scalable environments




How You Work

We are a fully remote team, and the way you work matters as much as what you build.

We expect you to be autonomous and reliable - managing your schedule, staying present during working hours, and engaging actively in calls and technical discussions.
Documentation-minded: You are comfortable translating ML concepts for less technical audiences, particularly when sharing research with Product.
Pragmatic by default: the goal is rarely the perfect model, but the right tradeoff between quality, scalability, and real-world constraints.
Team player: You communicate proactively and work well within a distributed, multicultural team




What's In It for You

A technology and media company with numerous branches worldwide (e.g. Paris, New York, São Paulo, Milan and Berlin)
Flexible workplace and working hours
A friendly and inclusive culture with equal opportunities for everyone
A place to grow and take initiative
One mental health day per year to focus on your well-being
Team events, winter parties, and more to celebrate our successes together
Four impact days per year to contribute positively to our environment and society




People at ShowHeroes

At ShowHeroes, we are committed to fair and objective decision-making across all stages of the employee lifecycle. This includes recruitment, hiring, promotion, and compensation processes.

We evaluate candidates and employees based on qualifications, performance, and alignment with our company values, regardless of gender, age, origin, religion, or disability.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Join the Magic

Our Mission

At Puratos, we create innovative food solutions for the health and well-being of people everywhere. As the global leader in bakery, patisserie and chocolate ingredients, we help our customers to be successful with their business, by turning technologies and experiences from food cultures around the world into new opportunities.

We are more than 10.000 employees in over 70 countries and a consolidated turnover of 4.3 billion euros. Our passion for innovation, a pioneering spirit and the unique Puratos culture drive our solid growth and vision for the future This, along with our commitment to our local communities, is what makes working at Puratos so magical.

To strengthen our Headquarters team in Groot Bijgaarden, we are looking for a:

Machine Learning Engineer

Job Summary: We are looking for a Machine Learning Engineer who thrives at the intersection of AI innovation, scalable data systems, and applied business impact.

This position will allow you to work on data engineering and data science use cases. As such, you will explore and generate value out of the company data by proactively getting information from various sources, analyzing them and build AI solutions that would improve and automate processes within the company and within the industry.

What will you do:

You will be involved in different analysis, development and research on many data projects:


Design, build, and deploy machine learning and GenAI solutions that drive innovation across our value chain, from R&D to production, marketing, and sustainability.
Partner with business and technical teams to translate complex problems into data-driven, deployable AI products.
Develop and optimize end-to-end ML pipelines in Databricks and Azure Machine Learning, ensuring scalability and robustness.
Apply graph data science techniques to reveal hidden connections across ingredients, recipes, and consumer insights.
Experiment with LLMs, embeddings, and AI copilots to accelerate decision-making and knowledge discovery within the company.
Contribute to our AI governance and best practices, ensuring reproducibility, observability, and ethical use of AI.
Collaborate with universities and research labs to push the boundaries of AI in food innovation.


Who are we looking for:

Qualifications


You are passionate about turning data into impact and curious about how AI can transform traditional industries.
You bring a strong technical foundation in data science, machine learning, and software engineering.
Master’s or PhD in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Statistics, Physics, or related fields.
3+ years of professional experience in applied ML or data engineering.
You are a team player who enjoys collaborating across disciplines


Technologies


Strong knowledge with: Python, SQL, and the data science ecosystem
Experience with Azure, Databricks, and Spark for scalable data processing and model deployment.
Familiarity with GenAI and LLM frameworks (OpenAI, Hugging Face, LangChain, etc.) Azure devops, Docker, FastApi, OpenWebUI, MCP, MLOps, Azure data factory, AML studio and AI Foundry is a strong plus.
Knowledge of graph databases (Neo4j) is an advantage.
Understanding of software engineering practices: version control, CI/CD (Git, MLflow, ...).


Language


Excellent communication skills in English; French or Dutch are assets.


What you can expect from Puratos?

You will join a fast-growing, stable family business that has become an international market leader with a corporate culture in which innovation and entrepreneurship are part of our main priorities. You will benefit from a great deal of freedom in your work, with plenty of room for initiative.

We at Puratos cultivate an inclusive global workplace, that fully embraces diversity and equal opportunities for all. We strive to reflect the richness of our customers, communities and society, accelerating our ability to innovate and deliver on our commitments.

You will be welcomed in a dynamic work environment where passion for innovation, team spirit and continuous improvement are key. You can count on a competitive salary package with attractive benefits such as:


Meal vouchers (9€/day)
Group, dental and hospitalization insurance fully paid by Puratos
Company Car
13th month pay
Minimum 5 training days per year
Responsibilities and the possibility to take ownership of your work

Watch our corporate movie and feel the Puratos Magic:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XNRsBzuRlNI

Working at Puratos is choosing a Top Employer in Belgium.

Shall we meet soon?

Title: Software Engineer
Description:
Most of the time, we deliver a standard version of Odoo to our customers. However, some of them are not satisfied with the product as it is presented and they wish to implement specific elements for a customized use of it (it can be from a simple button to add, to larger projects).

As Technical Consultant, you'll be responsible for developing customization of the solution, from technical analysis to development and support.




Customer relations are handled by our Business Analysts in the office next door and they present you the customization you have to bring. Your daily tasks will be undertaken from our office in Louvain-La-Neuve, you would then not have to move around to the clients' office. If you need more details from the customer, contacts will be handled by phone.







Programming Languages: Python & JavaScript
Database: PostgreSQL (with object relational mapping)
Collaboration platform: GitHub
Development model: Open with external community
Framework: Odoo (ORM, Workflows, Report Engine, BI, AI)




RESPONSIBILITIES

Develop solutions for our clients from many different industries, sectors and company size
Support and maintain those specific solutions
Tailor our SaaS offer
Support and train our partner network




MUST HAVE

Several programming languages
Object-oriented programming language, object modeling and relational database
Passion for development
Ability to learn and evolve quickly
Fluent in English
Team spirit







Please note we also have a very interesing position of Software Developer in Grand-Rosière (20min drive from Louvain-la-Neuve). Those developers will focus on the product itself and they don't work for customers.

Title: Mathematics Specialist - Fully Remote
Description:
About The Job

Mercor connects elite creative and technical talent with leading AI research labs. Headquartered in San Francisco, our investors include Benchmark, General Catalyst, Peter Thiel, Adam D'Angelo, Larry Summers, and Jack Dorsey.

Position: Expert Mathematician

Type: Contract

Compensation: $25–$60/hour

Location: Remote

Commitment: 10+ hours/week

Role Responsibilities


Author clear, unambiguous, open-ended mathematical prompts that elicit evaluable AI responses.
Verify prompts are within the scope of the assigned subdomain and correctly rated for difficulty.
Ensure all 5 prompts in a task are sufficiently distinct from one another with varying difficulty levels.
Apply expert judgment to assess the depth and quality of mathematical reasoning required.
Edit prompts and difficulty assignments where standards are not met.



Qualifications

Must-Have


Master's degree or higher in Mathematics, Applied Mathematics, Statistics, or a closely related field.
2–6 years of professional or research experience in a quantitative field.
Strong command of graduate-level mathematical concepts including proof writing, analysis, and formal reasoning.
Excellent written English and ability to craft precise, well-scoped technical questions.



Preferred


Experience in academic research, mathematical competition design, or quantitative industry roles.



Resources & Support


For details about the interview process and platform information, please check: https://talent.docs.mercor.com/welcome
For any help or support, reach out to: support@mercor.com



PS: Our team reviews applications daily. Please complete your AI interview and application steps to be considered for this opportunity.

Title: Data Scientist / ML Engineer
HTML:
Based in Portugal, Wire IT is your specialized IT consulting partner with 18 years of experience driven by an experienced and senior team that helps clients make the right decisions in a fast-moving market.

Wire IT's ambition is to grow while keeping true to its nature: agile, people-centered, and fun. As we like to say: “It’s not only what we do, it’s how we manage that truly make the difference.”.

If you're looking for more than just a job - if you're looking for a place where you can truly make a difference - you might be the next person to join WIRE IT.

Role: Data Scientist / ML Engineer

Location: Remote, Portugal

Required skills:

  • 4–5 years of professional experience in Data Science or Machine Learning.
  • Strong hands-on experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP).
  • Proven experience deploying ML models to production using Vertex AI.
  • Strong SQL skills, preferably with BigQuery.
  • Solid programming skills in Python (e.g., pandas, scikit-learn, XGBoost, TensorFlow, or PyTorch).
  • Experience building end-to-end ML workflows (data ingestion modeling deployment monitoring).
  • Strong communication skills with the ability to explain complex concepts to business stakeholders.


We offer:

  • No-term full-time contract;
  • Health Insurance;
  • 22 days of paid vacation;
  • 4 extra days annually (Carnival, Christmas Eve, New Year's Eve, Birthday);
  • Meal Allowance on card (Coverflex);
  • Referral Bonus;
  • Special discounts and flex options;
  • Training annual budget;
  • A great team-oriented culture;
  • Chance to become a part of a dynamically growing international company;
  • Challenging projects giving the unique opportunity to grow and learn.


Following the EU General Data Protection Regulation No. 2016/679, we inform you that by responding to this announcement, you consent to the use of your data by Wire IT, for as long as necessary. In case of doubt or request for elimination of your personal data, please send an email to info@wireit.pt

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Place: City Of London, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Machine Learning Engineer




An AI-driven start-up, backed by $20M in Series A funding, is looking for its first Machine Learning Engineer to help scale their product and infrastructure. The platform transforms educational content into smart, personalised learning tools - and is already seeing rapid user growth and strong traction in the market.




This is a unique opportunity to join early, influence the technical roadmap, and work directly with experienced founders and a senior engineering team.




What you'll be doing:

Designing, building and deploying ML-powered features across a production platform
Working across Python and TypeScript, integrating models into scalable, real-time systems
Fine-tuning and training NLP models using techniques like transformers
Driving the machine learning strategy and building best practices from the ground up
Collaborating closely with product, engineering, and leadership




What we're looking for:

Strong commercial experience building and deploying ML models in production
Proficiency in Python and familiarity with TypeScript/JavaScript
Experience with NLP or recommendation systems is a bonus
Excited by start-ups: product-minded, hands-on, and motivated by impact




What's on offer:

£100,000 - £120,000 salary + meaningful equity
4 days/week in a modern London office near Liverpool St
Greenfield ML ownership with direct access to founding team
A chance to shape the future of an AI product already loved by users

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
The Opportunity

As an Engineering Data Scientist, you will be part of Indian Operation Center, India (INOPC-PG), aiming to develop a global value chain, where key business activities, resources, and expertise are shared across geographic boundaries to optimize value for Hitachi Energy customers across markets. As part of Transformers BU, we provide high-quality engineering and Technology to Hitachi Energy world. This is an important step from Hitachi Energy's Global Footprint strategy.

How You’ll Make An Impact


Drive technical excellence in Data Science by spearheading high-impact ML initiatives within a cross-functional team of engineers and analysts.
Design and deploy advanced machine learning models and predictive frameworks, moving beyond descriptive reporting to provide foresight into business trends.
Analyze high-dimensional datasets to extract latent patterns and deliver prescriptive recommendations that drive strategic business optimization.
Partner with Data Engineers to architect robust pipelines and feature stores, ensuring high-quality data ingestion for model training and inference.
Ensuring model transparency, performance stability, and adherence to ethical AI governance and best practices.
Leverage tools like Power BI and Python to visualize complex model outputs, making machine learning results interpretable for executive decision-making.
Collaborate with business units to identify challenges solvable through AI, proposing end-to-end Data Science solutions that deliver measurable ROI.
Proactively troubleshoot and refine ML models, addressing data drift and performance degradation to maintain long-term accuracy and scalability.
Responsible to ensure compliance with applicable external and internal regulations, procedures, and guidelines.
Living Hitachi Energy’s core values safety and integrity, which means taking responsibility for your own actions while caring for your colleagues and the business.


Your Background


Bachelor’s degree in computer science / data science, or quantitative discipline. 4–8 years of progressive experience in Data Science and Advanced Analytics.
Extensive experience in Statistical Modeling and Machine Learning, including deep understanding of supervised/unsupervised learning, feature engineering, and model validation.
Expert-level proficiency in Complex SQL and architecting scalable ETL/ELT pipelines to handle high-volume, unstructured, and structured datasets.
Hands-on experience with Databricks (PySpark/Spark SQL) for large-scale data processing and Microsoft Power BI for delivering executive-level prescriptive insights. Should have Strong Domain Knowledge across the Data Science
Experience in Azure Databricks, Synapse, ADF, Azure ML Studio. Proficient in Python (Scikit-Learn, Pandas, NumPy) and seasoned in MLOps principles (model versioning, deployment, and monitoring) within an Agile framework. Ability to independently manage end-to-end BI projects.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a focus on data accuracy and performance optimization. Ability to collaborate effectively across engineering and business teams from different cultural and geographical backgrounds.
Strong analytical skills, experience in data analysis and management. Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
Detail-oriented with the ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously. Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines. Tasks and time self-management.
Proficiency in both spoken & written English language is required.


Hitachi Energy is a global technology leader in electrification, powering a sustainable energy future through innovative power grid technologies with digital at the core. Over three billion people depend on our technologies to power their daily lives.

With over a century in pioneering mission-critical technologies like high-voltage, transformers, automation, and power electronics, we are addressing the most urgent energy challenge of our time – balancing soaring electricity demand, while decarbonizing the power system.

Headquartered in Switzerland, we employ over 50,000 people in 60 countries and generate revenues of around $16 billion USD. We welcome you to apply today.

Title: Talent Acquisition Supervisor (Urgent Hiring)
Place: Muntinlupa City, National Capital Region, Philippines
Description:
Office location: Alabang, Muntinlupa

Work setup: Full onsite

Schedule: Monday to Friday (Compressed Work Schedule)

(7AM - 6PM every Monday; 7AM - 5:30 PM from Tuesday to Friday)

Role Overview

The Talent Acquisition Supervisor leads and oversees end-to-end recruitment activities, ensuring the timely and effective hiring of qualified candidates for both white-collar and support roles. This role is responsible for driving recruitment strategies, optimizing hiring processes, and guiding the recruitment team to meet organizational talent needs.

Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in Human Resources, Psychology, Business Administration, or a related field
Minimum of 3 years of experience in recruitment or talent acquisition, with at least 1 year in a supervisory or leadership role
Proven experience in end-to-end recruitment, including sourcing, screening, interviewing, and onboarding
Strong background in hiring for white-collar and support positions
Exposure to the warehousing or logistics industry is highly preferred
Excellent communication, interpersonal, and stakeholder management skills
Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple hiring requirements simultaneously
Willing to travel if needed.
Can start immediately.



Key Responsibilities


Supervise and manage the full-cycle recruitment process, from job requisition to onboarding
Lead sourcing strategies to attract qualified candidates for both professional (white-collar) and support roles
Screen, assess, and endorse candidates in alignment with role requirements and organizational standards
Collaborate closely with hiring managers to understand manpower needs and ensure timely fulfillment of roles
Monitor recruitment metrics (e.g., time-to-fill, quality of hire) and implement process improvements
Provide coaching and guidance to recruitment team members to enhance performance and capability
Ensure a positive candidate experience throughout the hiring journey
Maintain and strengthen talent pipelines for critical and recurring roles
Support employer branding initiatives and recruitment campaigns
Ensure compliance with company policies and recruitment best practices

Title: CRISPR Molecular Biologist (Gene Editing & AI Data) | $60/hr Remote
Description:
Position: Molecular Biologist

Type: Hourly contract

Compensation: $40 - $60/hour

Location: Remote

Commitment: 10–40 hours/week




Role Responsibilities

Design and optimize CRISPR-based gene editing experiments.
Develop cloning strategies to create constructs and cell lines.
Perform genotyping, PCR assays, and sequence analysis for validation.
Design DNA and RNA sequences for experimental and screening workflows.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to structure data for AI training.
Apply molecular biology techniques to study cellular and molecular systems.
Document experimental procedures and results with clarity and precision.




Requirements

Strong experience in CRISPR and gene editing workflows.
Strong experience in cloning, genotyping, and PCR-based assays.
Strong expertise in sequence design and molecular screening strategies.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with attention to detail.
Strong analytical skills in molecular biology and cellular systems.
Ability to work independently in remote, research-driven environments.
Degree in Molecular Biology or a related field.




Application Process (Takes 20 Min)

Easy Apply on LinkedIn
Check email for next steps
Participate in resume evaluation & interview stage

Title: ME Graduate
Description:
Forvia, a sustainable mobility technology leader

We pioneer technology for mobility experience that matter to people.

Your Mission, Roles And Responsibilities

Job title: Y.E.S Manufacturing Engineering (ME) Graduate

Location: Pretoria (Rosslyn)

Reporting to: ME Manager

Contract: 12 Months Contract

Closing Date: 30 April 2026

Main purpose of the role

The ME Graduate is responsible for ensuring an optimum process to support the production line, by Supporting the FMEA’s, process flow diagrams, standard manufacturing operations to produce parts that meet the customer specification at all times using Faurecia standards and tools. The key responsibility of ME Grad is to Maintain & Create & Audit accurate BOM & Master data and ensure smooth and efficient process execution.

The main missions will be to gain development and learning on the following:

As a ME Graduate, your main responsibilities are as follows:


Participate review process FMEA’s.
Participate review process flow diagrams.
Participate in SREA’s submissions.
Maintain manufacturing process documents for newly implemented machines.
Support processes by supporting continuous improvement and production through participation to the process specialist network.
Maintain BOM & Master data in line with cycle time in line with takt time and standard operations to establish manufacturing routines by releasing manufacturing process documents and process flowcharts.
Support in industrialisation trials and Run-at-Rate
Participate in plant lay-out update.
Ensure continuous improvement by capitalizing on lessons learnt and provide best practices using tools like; process productivity analysis, improvements action plans, production equipment performance, trouble shooting and problem-solving process.
Ensure FES & HSE standards & processes are always met/ followed.



Your profile and competencies to succeed

Qualifications And Position Requirements

Qualifications and experience requirements:


National Diploma equivalent qualification in Electrical / Mechanical / Industrial Engineering.
Experience in an automotive industry will be advantages.



The Ideal Graduate Should Have


Knowledge of productivity and Lean Management methodologies
Experience on computer systems
Excellent people and teamwork skills
Ability to work effectively within a matrix organisation
Strong communication and interpersonal skills
Basic understanding of supplier audit processes and quality management systems
Must be between 18 and 35 years of age
Must not have previously participated in the Y.E.S Youth Program
Proficient in speaking, reading, and writing English



Leadership competences: As an employee – promote Being Faurecia, Respect of Faurecia code of ethics (Entrepreneurial mindset, autonomy and accountability, fairness and respect) Knowledge of Employee Empowerment (EE) basics

What We Can Do For You


At Forvia, you will find an engaging and dynamic environment where you can contribute to the development of sustainable mobility leading technologies.
We are the seventh-largest global automotive supplier, employing more than 157,000 people in more than 40 countries which makes a lot of opportunity for career development.
We welcome energetic and agile people who can thrive in a fast-changing environment. People who share our strong values. Team players with a collaborative mindset and a passion to deliver high standards for our clients. Lifelong learners. High performers. Globally minded people who aspire to work in a transforming industry, where excellence, speed, and quality count.
We cultivate a learning environment, dedicating tools and resources to ensure we remain at the forefront of mobility. Our people enjoy an average of more than 22 hours of online and in-person training within FORVIA University (five campuses around the world)
We offer a multicultural environment that values diversity and international collaboration. We believe that diversity is a strength. To create an inclusive culture where all forms of diversity create real value for the company, we have adopted gender diversity targets and inclusion action plans.
Achieving CO2 Net Zero as a pioneer of the automotive industry is a priority: In June 2022, Forvia became the first global automotive group to be certified with the new SBTI Net-Zero Standard (the most ambitious standard of SBTi), aligned with the ambition of the 2015 Paris Agreement of limiting global warming to 1.5°C. Three principles guide our action: use less, use better and use longer, with a focus on recyclability and circular economy.



Why join us

FORVIA is an automotive technology group at the heart of smarter and more sustainable mobility. We bring together expertise in electronics, clean mobility, lighting, interiors, seating, and lifecycle solutions to drive change in the automotive industry.

With a history stretching back more than a century, we are the 7th largest global automotive supplier, employing more than 157,000 people in 43 countries. You'll find our technology in around 1 out of 2 vehicles produced anywhere in the world.

In June 2022, we became the 1st global automotive group to be certified with the SBTI Net-Zero Standard. We have committed to reach CO2 Net Zero by no later than 2045.

As technological innovation and the need for sustainability transform the automotive industry, we are ideally positioned to deliver solutions that will enhance the lives of road-users everywhere.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
About The Company

Capital One is a leading diversified financial services company renowned for its innovative approach to banking and credit card services. With a strong focus on technology-driven solutions, Capital One leverages data analytics, artificial intelligence, and machine learning to deliver personalized financial products and exceptional customer experiences. The company’s commitment to innovation, integrity, and inclusivity positions it as a forward-thinking organization dedicated to transforming the financial landscape. Operating across multiple regions, Capital One emphasizes a collaborative culture that fosters continuous learning, diversity, and growth, making it an ideal environment for professionals seeking to make a meaningful impact in the financial technology space.

About The Role

The Lead Machine Learning Engineer (MLOps, KServe, Kubernetes Clusters, PyTorch, TensorFlow on AWS) at Capital One is a pivotal role within the organization’s AI and data science ecosystem. As a key member of the Intelligent Foundations and Experiences (IFX) team, you will be responsible for designing, building, and deploying scalable machine learning systems that drive business value and operational efficiency. Your expertise will enable the team to produce high-quality, reliable, and explainable AI solutions that meet regulatory and ethical standards. This role involves collaborating with cross-functional teams, including data scientists, software engineers, and product managers, to translate complex models into production-ready applications that serve millions of customers. You will have the opportunity to lead initiatives that leverage cloud technologies, container orchestration, and advanced ML frameworks to create innovative solutions aligned with the company’s strategic objectives.

Qualifications

The ideal candidate will possess a combination of educational background and extensive experience in machine learning, data engineering, and cloud infrastructure. A bachelor’s degree in computer science, electrical engineering, mathematics, or a related field is required, with a preference for candidates holding a master’s or doctoral degree. Candidates should have a minimum of six years of experience designing and implementing data-intensive solutions using distributed computing frameworks, along with at least four years of programming experience in Python, Scala, or Java. Proven expertise in building, scaling, and optimizing machine learning systems is essential, along with hands-on experience deploying models in cloud environments such as AWS. Additionally, familiarity with industry-standard ML frameworks like PyTorch, TensorFlow, or scikit-learn, and a strong understanding of MLOps practices, including CI/CD pipelines, model monitoring, and governance, are highly desirable. Leadership experience, especially leading teams developing ML solutions, is preferred, along with contributions to industry research, open-source projects, or patents.

Responsibilities


Design, develop, and deploy scalable machine learning models and components that address real-world business challenges in collaboration with product and data science teams.
Architect and implement robust ML infrastructure, leveraging cloud platforms like AWS, and utilize container orchestration tools such as Kubernetes and KServe to facilitate deployment and management.
Create and optimize data pipelines to ensure efficient feeding of ML models, incorporating best practices for data gathering, cleaning, and feature engineering.
Develop, test, and validate application code and ML models, automating deployment processes to support continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines.
Monitor, retrain, and maintain models in production, ensuring high availability, resilience, and performance while adhering to responsible AI principles.
Collaborate within Agile teams to enhance software solutions, integrating ML components seamlessly into larger systems.
Ensure compliance with governance, security, and ethical standards related to AI and data usage, including model explainability and bias mitigation.
Leverage programming languages such as Python, Scala, or Java to develop performant and maintainable codebases, contributing to best practices and code reviews.
Stay current with emerging trends and innovations in machine learning, MLOps, and cloud technologies, applying new knowledge to improve existing systems and processes.


Benefits

Capital One offers a comprehensive benefits package designed to support the health, financial security, and overall well-being of its employees. This includes competitive salary packages, performance-based incentive compensation, and long-term incentives where applicable. Employees have access to health insurance plans, retirement savings options, paid time off, and wellness programs. The organization fosters a culture of continuous learning through training, professional development opportunities, and participation in industry conferences. Additionally, Capital One promotes a flexible work environment, diversity and inclusion initiatives, and resources that support work-life balance. The company also provides various employee assistance programs and resources to ensure a supportive and engaging workplace.

Equal Opportunity

Capital One is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive environment for all employees and applicants. The company adheres to all applicable federal, state, and local laws regarding non-discrimination and equal employment opportunities. Capital One values diversity and is dedicated to creating a workplace free from discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic. The organization actively promotes a culture of respect, fairness, and opportunity, ensuring that all individuals have equal access to employment, advancement, and development opportunities. Capital One also complies with applicable laws related to criminal background checks and provides reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities or other needs during the hiring process and employment.

Title: Commercial(e) F/H
Description:
L'entreprise

Dynamique et séduisante, chaleureuse et envoûtante, la ville de Montpellier ne laisse personne indifférent. En son cœur, à deux pas de la place de la Comédie et à 2 minutes de la gare, se dresse une majestueuse bâtisse du XVIIIe. Notre hôtel spa associe charme et caractère dans une ambiance luxueuse et élégante. Majestueux tout simplement !

Description Du Poste

Nous sommes à la recherche d'un(e) Commercial(e) passionné(e) et motivé(e) pour contribuer au développement de notre établissement.

Rattaché(e) à La Direction, Vous Aurez Pour Missions


Développement du portefeuille client : Fidéliser et développer un réseau d'entreprises, agences de voyages, et partenaires stratégiques.
Prospection active : Identifier et séduire de nouveaux clients potentiels, tout en décelant des opportunités sur des segments de marché porteurs.
Élaboration d'offres sur-mesure : Adapter et personnaliser les propositions commerciales selon les besoins spécifiques de chaque client.
Participation à des événements professionnels : Représenter notre hôtel lors de salons et d'événements pour renforcer notre visibilité et nos relations commerciales.
Collaboration avec les équipes internes : Travailler main dans la main avec les équipes opérationnelles pour garantir une expérience client optimale.
Analyse de la concurrence et suivi du marché : Assurer une veille concurrentielle et anticiper les tendances du secteur pour rester compétitif.
Gestion des demandes de groupes : Gérer les demandes clients de la qualification à la contractualisation.


Profil recherché

Profil Recherché


Vous justifiez d'une première expérience réussi à un poste similaire (1 an d'expérience minimum).
Anglais professionnel requis.
Autonomie, rigueur et forte orientation client. Vous savez gérer plusieurs priorités avec aisance.

Title: Expert in AI/ NLP/ ML
Description:
Description of the Tasks


Following tasks will be performed by the external service provider:

Design the IT architecture for solutions in the NLP / ML / AI fields, and implement PoCs such as in the ChatBot / RAG field;

Analyse data assets & business processes;

Analyse risk-level of existing LLMs implemented in few of EMPL IT systems;

Industrialise PoCs once they have proven to bring value-added, in close collaboration with other engineers members of EMPL.A4;

Train custom machine learning (e.g. RAG based) based on structured and unstructured data;

Interact with the local data correspondent, the digital transformation officer, and other IT stakeholders;

Process, cleanse, and verify the integrity of data used for analysis;

Follow the progress of implementation of corporate AI initiatives @EC, and identify opportunities to leverage the use of them within DG EMPL;

Contribute to the analysis of data management vision, strategy and policy and derive the IT requirements.




Requirements:

Excellent knowledge of Python

Excellent knowledge of NLP/ML and common Python libraries (e.g.: PyTorch, SpaCy, NLTK, Scikit-learn, Pandas, Pydantic)

Very good knowledge of package management (e.g.: Poetry, pip, Conda)

Very good knowledge of OOP and functional programming

Very good knowledge of Git

Extensive knowledge of machine learning techniques and algorithms

Extensive knowledge of neural networks and deep learning, with a focus on text processing models

Very good knowledge of prompt engineering techniques

Very good knowledge of models MLOps techniques (model testing/deployment/monitoring/versioning, experiment tracking)

Very good knowledge of Data Management

Good knowledge of statistical analysis, experimental design and large scale data processing

Good knowledge of LLMs and Transformer architecture

Good knowledge of RAG systems

Good knowledge of Azure and/or AWS

Good knowledge of Docker, Kubernetes and IaC (e.g.: knowledge of Bicep and/or Terraform will be considered a plus)

Good knowledge of Linux

Good knowledge of NoSQL databases (e.g.: MongoDB, ElasticSearch)

Good knowledge of vector DB

Good knowledge of query languages (e.g.: SQL, ES, QL)

Good knowledge of agile software development methodologies



Skills:

Ability to participate in multilingual meetings

Ability to understand, speak and write English, optionally French as an additional asset

Excellent interpersonal and communication skills

Ability to work in a team as well as autonomously

Results-oriented mindset, focused on delivering

Good applied statistics skills, such as distributions, statistical testing, regression, etc.

Good scripting and programming skills

Data-driven mindset



Non-Technical Skills:

Capability of integration in an international/multicultural environment, rapid self-starting capability and experience in working in a team

Ability to participate in multilingual meetings

Ability to work in multi-cultural environment, on multiple large projects

Excellent Team Player



Specific Expertise:

Any experience in the field of RAG processing will be considered as an asset

Title: Assistant de Direction F/H
Description:
L'entreprise

Relevant du secteur de l’économie sociale, le Groupe ACPPA, rassemble près de 2700 hommes et femmes passionnés, investis dans leur métier qui partagent des valeurs fortes. Implantés sur le territoire national, ils accompagnent chaque jour plus de 6 400 personnes âgées.

Notre Groupe conforte son développement au travers de 2 activités d'expertise : l'accueil et l'accompagnement des personnes âgées du domicile à l'institution, la formation et le conseil.

Le siège social, où vous exercerez votre activité est situé dans un magnifique parc arboré sur les hauteurs de Francheville. Desservi par les transports en commun (bus ; train) et pourvu d'un parking réservé au personnel.

Consultez notre blog : Le Blog - DU GROUPE ACPPA

Description Du Poste

Rattaché(e) à la Directrice médicale, vous assurez un rôle central dans l’organisation et le bon fonctionnement de la Direction Médicale du Groupe ACPPA. Véritable interface entre les membres de la Direction médicale, les établissements et les services du Siège social, vous contribuez activement aux démarches qualité, gestion des risques et aux projets structurants du Groupe.

Vos Missions Assistanat De Direction


Organisation et coordination du fonctionnement de la Direction médicale
Traitement du courrier et diffusion des informations auprès des établissements et services du siège
Gestion des communications téléphoniques
Gestion de l’agenda de la Directrice médicale (planification de rendez-vous, réunions, arbitrages)
Organisation logistique des déplacements au sein des structures du Groupe (projet AEGIS, réunions métiers), dans le respect de la politique voyages Groupe
Gestion des inscriptions aux colloques, abonnements et événements professionnels
Saisie, suivi et classement administratif (congés, notes de frais, documents divers)


Organisation et logistique des réunions du processus médical :


Réservation des salles et planification des réunions
Convocation des participants
Préparation et diffusion des ordres du jour
Prise de notes assistée par IA et rédaction des comptes rendus
Suivi et coordination des actions décidées en réunion


Suivi des dossiers portés par la Direction médicale :


Compilation des Rapports Médicaux Annuels (RAMA)
Suivi du recueil des données et indicateurs Hébergement & Soins, enquêtes nutrition Groupe
Suivi des indicateurs hebdomadaires (soins, hygiène, canicule, etc.)
Gestion administrative des formations (inscriptions, relances, évaluations, conventions, facturation)
Suivi administratif et comptable des dossiers de subvention
Suivi des audits, intégrations et tutorats


Missions transverses et projets :

Selon les besoins du service, vous pourrez être amené(e) à :


Participer au pilotage du projet gérontologique Groupe
Réaliser une veille réglementaire soins et hygiène, notamment via des outils IA, et en assurer le partage auprès des équipes
Contribuer à la gestion et à l’actualisation de la base documentaire de la Direction médicale


Modalités Du Poste


Poste en CDI,
0.5 ETP,
Localisation du poste : 7 chemin du GAREIZIN - 69 340 FRANCHEVILLE.
Jours de présence sur site à organiser avec la Directrice médicale,
Rémunération annuelle brute comprise entre 14k€ et 15k€ (prime de fin d'année icluse).


Si vous aspirez à rejoindre une équipe unie et bienveillante, réunie autour de valeurs et d’un projet humain, rejoignez-nous !

Profil recherché

Profil Recherché


Formation en assistanat de direction / assistanat médico-social ou équivalent
Expérience réussie sur un poste similaire, idéalement au sein d’un environnement sanitaire, médico-social ou institutionnel
Excellentes capacités organisationnelles, rigueur et sens des priorités
Très bonnes qualités rédactionnelles et relationnelles
Aisance avec les outils bureautiques (pack office) et collaboratifs ; appétence pour les outils numériques et l’IA appréciée
Discrétion, fiabilité et sens de la confidentialité indispensables


En intégrant nos équipes, vous bénéficiez :


D’un accompagnement par un tuteur sur toute la durée de votre intégration au sein de l’ACPPA
D’un parcours de formation valorisant et qualitatif grâce au Pôle Formation Santé du Groupe (e-learning, présentiel, serious game…)


Nous Rejoindre C’est Aussi Profiter


D’une mutuelle d’entreprise,
D’une reprise d’ancienneté professionnelle,
Des avantages d’un Comité Social et Economique,
De repas à des prix avantageux,
D’actions diverses dans le cadre de notre politique engagée pour une bonne Qualité de Vie au Travail,
Des avantages de notre CSE,
D'un Plan de Mobilité Durable,
D'un Groupe d’envergure nationale avec plus de 40 ans d’expertise en gérontologie offrant de nombreuses possibilités d’évolution interne.

En intégrant nos équipes, vous bénéficiez :

  • D’un accompagnement par un tuteur sur toute la durée de votre intégration au sein de l’ACPPA
  • D’un parcours de formation valorisant et qualitatif grâce au Pôle Formation Santé du Groupe (e-learning, présentiel, serious game…)

Nous Rejoindre C’est Aussi Profiter

  • D’une mutuelle d’entreprise,
  • D’une reprise d’ancienneté professionnelle,
  • Des avantages d’un Comité Social et Economique,
  • De repas à des prix avantageux,
  • D’actions diverses dans le cadre de notre politique engagée pour une bonne Qualité de Vie au Travail,
  • Des avantages de notre CSE,
  • D'un Plan de Mobilité Durable,
  • D'un Groupe d’envergure nationale avec plus de 40 ans d’expertise en gérontologie offrant de nombreuses possibilités d’évolution interne.

Title: Bioinformatics Specialist | Upto $85/hr
Description:
About The Job

Mercor connects elite creative and technical talent with leading AI research labs. Headquartered in San Francisco, our investors include Benchmark, General Catalyst, Peter Thiel, Adam D'Angelo, Larry Summers, and Jack Dorsey.

Position: Bioinformatician

Type: Contract

Compensation: $60–$85/hour

Duration: 2-week engagement

Role Responsibilities


Review representative problems from your assigned problem class to understand the data shape and expected output.
Specify a complete toolkit including Python/R packages and system tools, each with a brief rationale.
Validate the final toolkit to ensure it solves specific classes of computational biology problems.
Shape how frontier models approach real scientific problems through your tool selections.
Work independently and collaboratively to meet project deadlines.



Qualifications

Must-Have


PhD or Master's degree in bioinformatics, computational biology, genomics, or a closely related field.
Hands-on experience with at least 3 of the following: protein structure analysis, variant effect prediction, RNA-seq/expression analysis, ChIP-seq/epigenomics, single-cell analysis, CRISPR screen analysis, molecular docking, or cheminformatics.
Deep familiarity with the standard tool ecosystem for your subdomain (e.g., Rosetta, FoldX, DESeq2, MAGeCK, AutoDock Vina, HOMER, scanpy).
Proficiency in Python and/or R for bioinformatics workflows.
Familiarity with command-line bioinformatics tools (e.g., samtools, BEDTools, BLAST).



Interview Process


Short screening questionnaire to assess subdomain expertise and tool familiarity.



Application Process (Takes 20–30 mins to complete)


Upload resume
AI interview based on your resume
Submit form



Resources & Support


For details about the interview process and platform information, please check: https://talent.docs.mercor.com/welcome
For any help or support, reach out to: support@mercor.com



PS: Our team reviews applications daily. Please complete your AI interview and application steps to be considered for this opportunity.

Title: Machine Learning/Software Engineer
Description:
ABOUT MULTUS

Our mission is to transform human and planetary health by designing cell culture media to fuel the industrial bioeconomy.




We serve companies in the cellular agriculture, advanced therapies and the biopharmaceutical sectors, developing and supplying cell culture media to accelerate their transition from lab to market.

If you are passionate about driving the growth of a sustainable bioeconomy we encourage you to apply and play your part in creating a better future for people and the planet!




ABOUT THE ROLE

This internship offers the opportunity to contribute to a high-impact R&D project at the intersection of machine learning, software engineering, and biotechnology. The work supports the development of computational tools that enhance how we collect, process, and learn from experimental data — helping to improve data quality, consistency, and the speed of our optimisation workflows.




Interns will explore and develop methods that combine software engineering with machine learning to build robust, reusable analytical pipelines. The project involves working with rich experimental datasets and developing approaches to extract reliable, quantitative insights from complex biological data.




This is a genuine blend of software engineering (building well-architected, maintainable systems) and data science/ML (training and evaluating models), making it ideal for candidates who enjoy working across both disciplines.




The role is ideal for students with a strong interest in applied machine learning, software engineering, or data science — particularly in scientific or industrial research contexts.




The internship is a paid fixed-term full-time contract for a minimum of three months, with a flexible start date in summer 2026. We welcome applications from students and recent graduates from any university.




RESPONSIBILITIES

Exploring and evaluating a range of machine learning and analytical approaches to extract quantitative insights from complex biological data
Building reproducible computational pipelines for training, evaluating, and deploying ML models
Working with deep learning architectures and exploring the use of transfer learning to adapt models across different experimental contexts
Developing tooling and infrastructure to support standardised, repeatable analytical workflows
Working with large-scale experimental datasets
Collaborating with cross-functional teams to integrate computational outputs into experimental workflows




SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

Proficiency in Python, with experience writing clean, well-structured, and maintainable code
Familiarity with machine learning and deep learning frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, or Keras
Proficiency in data manipulation and analysis, including data cleaning, feature engineering, and model evaluation
Experience with version control systems like Git, supporting collaboration and efficient code management
Strong foundation in mathematics and statistics, particularly linear algebra, calculus, and probability theory
Excellent problem-solving and critical thinking abilities, with a capacity to work both independently and as part of a team
Strong communication skills for presenting complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders
Ability to demonstrate initiative, proactive problem-solving, and comfort with ambiguity
Enthusiasm for contributing to a fast-paced and interdisciplinary environment, with an eye for detail and code quality
Passion for learning new technologies and a strong curiosity to explore unknown areas
Right to work in the UK




The following skills and experiences would help you stand out even more:

Experience with computer vision or image processing (e.g. OpenCV, scikit-image, or similar)
Familiarity with transfer learning and fine-tuning pre-trained models on domain-specific data
Experience building data pipelines or working with large-scale datasets
Understanding of software engineering best practices: testing, CI/CD, containerisation (e.g. Docker)
Exposure to MLOps tooling for experiment tracking and model management (e.g. MLflow, Weights & Biases)




OUR HIRING PROCESS

Application Closes: Rolling - we will review the applications on a rolling basis
Round 0. Apply online
Round 1. Task
Round 2. 60-minute panel interview to discuss the task and interests




BENEFITS

Salary
25 days' holiday + flexible bank holidays (pro-rata)
Flexible working hours
Collaborative and driven team
Company socials and office snacks




OUR VALUES

TENACITY – WE TRY THINGS WITHOUT THE FEAR OF FAILURE

COLLABORATION – WE WORK TOGETHER INCLUSIVELY, CONSTRUCTIVELY & EFFICIENTLY

CANDOUR – WE TALK TO EACH OTHER WITH RESPECT AND INTEGRITY




OUR ED&I STATEMENT

Multus is an equal-opportunity employer committed to providing a working environment that embraces inclusion, diversity, and belonging. At Multus, we are committed to creating an environment where everyone feels they belong, are enabled to thrive, are respected for who they are, know that they matter, and are empowered to contribute meaningfully. We believe that diverse perspectives and inclusive collaboration drive innovation and make us stronger together.
